terms.short_def: Large Language Model.
terms.full_def: Large Language Model. A neural network with billions of parameters trained on large text corpora to predict and generate language.

terms.short_def: Neural network architecture introduced by Vaswani et al. (2017) that replaces recurrence with self-attention, allowing parallel processing…
terms.full_def: Neural network architecture introduced by Vaswani et al. (2017) that replaces recurrence with self-attention, allowing parallel processing of sequences.

terms.short_def: The original Transformer layout: an encoder processes the full input bidirectionally, a decoder generates output autoregressively, with cro…
terms.full_def: The original Transformer layout: an encoder processes the full input bidirectionally, a decoder generates output autoregressively, with cross-attention connecting them.

terms.short_def: Simplified Transformer architecture (GPT, LLaMA) that removes the encoder and cross-attention, using only masked self-attention and FFN lay…
terms.full_def: Simplified Transformer architecture (GPT, LLaMA) that removes the encoder and cross-attention, using only masked self-attention and FFN layers.

terms.short_def: Recurrent Neural Network.
terms.full_def: Recurrent Neural Network. Processes sequences one step at a time, maintaining a hidden state. Predecessor to the Transformer.

terms.short_def: Long Short-Term Memory.
terms.full_def: Long Short-Term Memory. An RNN variant with gating mechanisms to better preserve long-range dependencies.

terms.short_def: Google's encoder-decoder Transformer (Raffel et al., 2019) that frames every NLP task as text-to-text.
terms.full_def: Google's encoder-decoder Transformer (Raffel et al., 2019) that frames every NLP task as text-to-text. Originator of several conventions reused elsewhere (e.g., SentencePiece vocab, relative positional bias).

terms.short_def: Google's 540B-parameter decoder-only LLM (Chowdhery et al., 2022).
terms.full_def: Google's 540B-parameter decoder-only LLM (Chowdhery et al., 2022). Uses SwiGLU, RoPE, parallel attention/FFN layers. Reference point for scaling experiments.

terms.short_def: Open-weights decoder-only LLM family from Mistral AI (Jiang et al., 2023).
terms.full_def: Open-weights decoder-only LLM family from Mistral AI (Jiang et al., 2023). 7B variant uses sliding-window attention and grouped-query attention on top of the LLaMA-style architecture.

terms.short_def: Anthropic's proprietary decoder-only LLM family.
terms.full_def: Anthropic's proprietary decoder-only LLM family. No public architecture paper; structural family shares the decoder-only Transformer lineage.

terms.short_def: Converting raw text into a sequence of discrete integer token IDs that a model can process.
terms.full_def: Converting raw text into a sequence of discrete integer token IDs that a model can process.

terms.short_def: Subword tokenization algorithm that iteratively merges the most frequent adjacent character pairs to build a vocabulary.
terms.full_def: Subword tokenization algorithm that iteratively merges the most frequent adjacent character pairs to build a vocabulary.

terms.short_def: A token that may be a whole word, a word fragment, or a single character/byte, depending on corpus frequency.
terms.full_def: A token that may be a whole word, a word fragment, or a single character/byte, depending on corpus frequency.

terms.short_def: A tokenizer that operates on raw byte sequences rather than Unicode characters, used by LLaMA.
terms.full_def: A tokenizer that operates on raw byte sequences rather than Unicode characters, used by LLaMA. Provides byte-fallback so any input can be tokenized.

terms.short_def: Mechanism ensuring no "unknown token" failures: any byte sequence can be represented, even if unseen during training.
terms.full_def: Mechanism ensuring no "unknown token" failures: any byte sequence can be represented, even if unseen during training.

terms.short_def: The fixed set of all tokens a model can recognize.
terms.full_def: The fixed set of all tokens a model can recognize. Size ranges from ~32K (LLaMA) to ~50K (GPT-3).

terms.short_def: A learned lookup table ($W_e \in \mathbb{R}^{V \times d_{\text{model}}}$) mapping each token index to a dense vector.
terms.full_def: A learned lookup table ($W_e \in \mathbb{R}^{V \times d_{\text{model}}}$) mapping each token index to a dense vector.

terms.short_def: Sharing the same weight matrix $W_e$ for both input embeddings and the output projection to logits, halving vocabulary-related parameters.
terms.full_def: Sharing the same weight matrix $W_e$ for both input embeddings and the output projection to logits, halving vocabulary-related parameters.

terms.short_def: Any mechanism that injects sequence-position information into the model, since self-attention is inherently permutation-invariant.
terms.full_def: Any mechanism that injects sequence-position information into the model, since self-attention is inherently permutation-invariant.

terms.short_def: Fixed positional encoding using sine/cosine functions at different frequencies (original Transformer, 2017).
terms.full_def: Fixed positional encoding using sine/cosine functions at different frequencies (original Transformer, 2017).

terms.short_def: A trainable position matrix $W_p$ where each row corresponds to a sequence position (GPT-1, 2018).
terms.full_def: A trainable position matrix $W_p$ where each row corresponds to a sequence position (GPT-1, 2018). Fixes maximum context length at training time.

terms.short_def: Positional encoding that rotates query and key vectors by position-dependent angles, making attention dot products depend on relative posit…
terms.full_def: Positional encoding that rotates query and key vectors by position-dependent angles, making attention dot products depend on relative position. Applied at every layer to Q and K only. Used by LLaMA and most modern LLMs.

terms.short_def: Mechanism where each position in a sequence computes a weighted sum over all positions, with weights determined by learned query-key simila…
terms.full_def: Mechanism where each position in a sequence computes a weighted sum over all positions, with weights determined by learned query-key similarity.

terms.short_def: Core attention operation: $\text{softmax}(QK^\top / \sqrt{d_k}) V$.
terms.full_def: Core attention operation: $\text{softmax}(QK^\top / \sqrt{d_k}) V$. Scaling by $\sqrt{d_k}$ prevents large dot products from saturating the softmax.

terms.short_def: Running $h$ parallel attention operations on different learned projections, then concatenating and projecting the results.
terms.full_def: Running $h$ parallel attention operations on different learned projections, then concatenating and projecting the results. Allows attending to different representation subspaces simultaneously.

terms.short_def: Projected representation of the position that is "asking" for information in attention.
terms.full_def: Projected representation of the position that is "asking" for information in attention.

terms.short_def: Projected representation of positions being "queried" against.
terms.full_def: Projected representation of positions being "queried" against. The Q·K dot product determines attention weights.

terms.short_def: Projected representation of the content to be aggregated.
terms.full_def: Projected representation of the content to be aggregated. Weighted by attention scores to produce the output.

terms.short_def: The softmax-normalized scores ($a_j \geq 0$, $\sum a_j = 1$) that determine how much each position contributes to the output.
terms.full_def: The softmax-normalized scores ($a_j \geq 0$, $\sum a_j = 1$) that determine how much each position contributes to the output.

terms.short_def: A triangular mask that prevents position $i$ from attending to positions $> i$, enforcing autoregressive (left-to-right) generation.
terms.full_def: A triangular mask that prevents position $i$ from attending to positions $> i$, enforcing autoregressive (left-to-right) generation.

terms.short_def: Attention where Q comes from the decoder and K, V come from the encoder output.
terms.full_def: Attention where Q comes from the decoder and K, V come from the encoder output. Removed in decoder-only models.

terms.short_def: Dimensionality of query/key and value projections per attention head.
terms.full_def: Dimensionality of query/key and value projections per attention head. Typically $d_{\text{model}} / h$.

terms.short_def: The Vaswani 2017 default: $h$ independent heads, each with its own $W_i^Q, W_i^K, W_i^V$.
terms.full_def: The Vaswani 2017 default: $h$ independent heads, each with its own $W_i^Q, W_i^K, W_i^V$. KV cache size per token per layer is $2 n_h d_h$ elements

terms.short_def: Variant where all heads share a single $W^K, W^V$ (only $W^Q$ remains per-head).
terms.full_def: Variant where all heads share a single $W^K, W^V$ (only $W^Q$ remains per-head). KV cache shrinks by factor $h$. Cheaper per-token decode at the cost of capacity and stability

terms.short_def: Interpolation between MHA and MQA: query heads are partitioned into $g$ groups, each group shares one $W^K, W^V$.
terms.full_def: Interpolation between MHA and MQA: query heads are partitioned into $g$ groups, each group shares one $W^K, W^V$. GQA-1 = MQA; GQA-$h$ = MHA. KV cache per token is $2 n_g d_h$ elements

terms.short_def: The Ainslie 2023 recipe for converting an existing MHA checkpoint to GQA/MQA: mean-pool the $W^K, W^V$ matrices within each group, then con…
terms.full_def: The Ainslie 2023 recipe for converting an existing MHA checkpoint to GQA/MQA: mean-pool the $W^K, W^V$ matrices within each group, then continue pre-training for $\alpha \approx 5\%$ of the original budget

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-V2's compression scheme.
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-V2's compression scheme. Rather than sharing K/V across heads, K and V are reconstructed at use time from a small latent $\mathbf{c}_t^{KV} = W^{DKV} \mathbf{h}_t \in \mathbb{R}^{d_c}$ via up-projections $W^{UK}, W^{UV}$. Cache holds only the latent. During inference $W^{UK}$ can be absorbed into $W^Q$ and $W^{UV}$ into $W^O$

terms.short_def: The MLA add-on that splits each query/key into a content part (no RoPE, low-rank reconstructed) and a small shared RoPE-carrying part.
terms.full_def: The MLA add-on that splits each query/key into a content part (no RoPE, low-rank reconstructed) and a small shared RoPE-carrying part. Necessary because standard RoPE inserts a position-dependent rotation between $W^Q$ and $W^{UK}$ that prevents the cache-absorption optimization

terms.short_def: Exact attention computed with re-organized memory access: tile $K, V$ into SRAM-resident blocks and use **online softmax** to combine block…
terms.full_def: Exact attention computed with re-organized memory access: tile $K, V$ into SRAM-resident blocks and use **online softmax** to combine block results, never materializing the $N \times N$ attention matrix in HBM. Forward + backward IO complexity $\Theta(N^2 d^2 M^{-1})$ vs.\ $\Theta(N d + N^2)$ for the standard implementation

terms.short_def: Numerically stable streaming softmax: maintain running max $m$ and normalizer $\ell$ across blocks; when a new block arrives, rescale the e…
terms.full_def: Numerically stable streaming softmax: maintain running max $m$ and normalizer $\ell$ across blocks; when a new block arrives, rescale the existing partial sum by $e^{m_\text{old} - m_\text{new}}$ before adding the block's contribution. Enables block-wise softmax without seeing the full row

terms.short_def: Designing an algorithm with explicit accounting for reads and writes between memory hierarchy levels (HBM ↔ SRAM on a GPU).
terms.full_def: Designing an algorithm with explicit accounting for reads and writes between memory hierarchy levels (HBM ↔ SRAM on a GPU). The principle behind FlashAttention

terms.short_def: Restructuring a computation to operate on sub-blocks that fit in fast memory.
terms.full_def: Restructuring a computation to operate on sub-blocks that fit in fast memory. Used in FlashAttention to keep $Q, K, V$ blocks in SRAM

terms.short_def: Storing only the output and softmax statistics from the forward pass and recomputing the attention matrix on-chip during the backward pass,…
terms.full_def: Storing only the output and softmax statistics from the forward pass and recomputing the attention matrix on-chip during the backward pass, trading FLOPs for memory

terms.short_def: The large, slow memory tier on a GPU (~40–80 GB on A100, 1.5–2.0 TB/s).
terms.full_def: The large, slow memory tier on a GPU (~40–80 GB on A100, 1.5–2.0 TB/s). Most operations in standard attention are HBM-bound

terms.short_def: The small, fast memory tier on a GPU (~192 KB per A100 SM, ~19 TB/s).
terms.full_def: The small, fast memory tier on a GPU (~192 KB per A100 SM, ~19 TB/s). FlashAttention restructures attention to keep working sets in SRAM

terms.short_def: Yuan et al.\ 2025: hardware-aligned natively sparse attention trained end-to-end (sparsity is part of pre-training, not retrofitted).
terms.full_def: Yuan et al.\ 2025: hardware-aligned natively sparse attention trained end-to-end (sparsity is part of pre-training, not retrofitted). Aimed at long context. Treated in `kb/notes/architecture/long-context.md`

terms.short_def: A position-wise two-layer (or three-layer for GLU variants) fully-connected network applied independently at each sequence position.
terms.full_def: A position-wise two-layer (or three-layer for GLU variants) fully-connected network applied independently at each sequence position. Transforms representations within each position, complementing attention which mixes across positions.

terms.short_def: Rectified Linear Unit: $\max(0, x)$.
terms.full_def: Rectified Linear Unit: $\max(0, x)$. Original Transformer activation (2017).

terms.short_def: Smooth activation $x \cdot \Phi(x)$ where $\Phi$ is the standard normal CDF.
terms.full_def: Smooth activation $x \cdot \Phi(x)$ where $\Phi$ is the standard normal CDF. Used by GPT-1 (2018).

terms.short_def: Activation function $x \cdot \sigma(\beta x)$ where $\sigma$ is the sigmoid.
terms.full_def: Activation function $x \cdot \sigma(\beta x)$ where $\sigma$ is the sigmoid. SiLU is the $\beta = 1$ case: $x \cdot \sigma(x)$. Unlike ReLU, it is smooth and non-monotonic around zero.

terms.short_def: The expanded hidden size inside the FFN.
terms.full_def: The expanded hidden size inside the FFN. Typically $4 \times d_{\text{model}}$, or $\frac{2}{3} \times 4 \times d_{\text{model}}$ for SwiGLU.

terms.short_def: Adding the sub-layer input directly to its output ($\text{output} = x + \text{SubLayer}(x)$).
terms.full_def: Adding the sub-layer input directly to its output ($\text{output} = x + \text{SubLayer}(x)$). Enables gradient flow and makes each layer learn a correction rather than a full representation.

terms.short_def: Normalizes across the feature dimension per position: subtract mean, divide by standard deviation, apply learned scale ($\gamma$) and bias…
terms.full_def: Normalizes across the feature dimension per position: subtract mean, divide by standard deviation, apply learned scale ($\gamma$) and bias ($\beta$).

terms.short_def: Simplified LayerNorm that drops mean-centering and bias, normalizing only by the RMS of the input.
terms.full_def: Simplified LayerNorm that drops mean-centering and bias, normalizing only by the RMS of the input. 7–64% faster with comparable quality. Used by LLaMA.

terms.short_def: Original Transformer placement: normalize after the residual addition.
terms.full_def: Original Transformer placement: normalize after the residual addition. Requires learning rate warmup for stable training.

terms.short_def: Modern placement: normalize before the sub-layer, then add residually.
terms.full_def: Modern placement: normalize before the sub-layer, then add residually. Gradients scale as $O(d\sqrt{\ln d / L})$, enabling stable training without warmup.

terms.short_def: Gradually increasing the learning rate from zero during early training steps.
terms.full_def: Gradually increasing the learning rate from zero during early training steps. Critical for Post-LN; unnecessary for Pre-LN.

terms.short_def: Unnormalized log-probabilities over the vocabulary, produced by the output projection ($h_{\text{final}} \cdot W_e^\top$).
terms.full_def: Unnormalized log-probabilities over the vocabulary, produced by the output projection ($h_{\text{final}} \cdot W_e^\top$).

terms.short_def: Converts logits to a probability distribution: $P(j) = e^{z_j} / \sum_k e^{z_k}$.
terms.full_def: Converts logits to a probability distribution: $P(j) = e^{z_j} / \sum_k e^{z_k}$.

terms.short_def: Training objective: $\mathcal{L} = -\log P(t_i \mid t_1, \ldots, t_{i-1})$, summed over all positions.
terms.full_def: Training objective: $\mathcal{L} = -\log P(t_i \mid t_1, \ldots, t_{i-1})$, summed over all positions.

terms.short_def: Generating tokens one at a time, left to right, where each prediction depends only on previous tokens.
terms.full_def: Generating tokens one at a time, left to right, where each prediction depends only on previous tokens.

terms.short_def: $2^{\mathcal{L}}$ (or $e^{\mathcal{L}}$ with natural log).
terms.full_def: $2^{\mathcal{L}}$ (or $e^{\mathcal{L}}$ with natural log). Measures how "surprised" the model is by the data. Lower is better.

terms.short_def: The model's hidden dimension (width of the residual stream).
terms.full_def: The model's hidden dimension (width of the residual stream). All sub-layers input and output this size.

terms.short_def: Sequence length (number of tokens in the input).
terms.full_def: Sequence length (number of tokens in the input).

terms.short_def: Number of attention heads.
terms.full_def: Number of attention heads.

terms.short_def: Number of Transformer layers (blocks).
terms.full_def: Number of Transformer layers (blocks).

terms.short_def: Vocabulary size.
terms.full_def: Vocabulary size.

terms.short_def: The $d_{\text{model}}$-dimensional vector representation at each position, flowing through the residual stream across layers.
terms.full_def: The $d_{\text{model}}$-dimensional vector representation at each position, flowing through the residual stream across layers.

terms.short_def: The learned weights of the model (embedding matrices, projection matrices, normalization scales, etc.).
terms.full_def: The learned weights of the model (embedding matrices, projection matrices, normalization scales, etc.).

terms.short_def: Selecting the highest-probability token at each step.
terms.full_def: Selecting the highest-probability token at each step.

terms.short_def: Sampling from only the $k$ most probable tokens.
terms.full_def: Sampling from only the $k$ most probable tokens.

terms.short_def: Sampling from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds $p$.
terms.full_def: Sampling from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds $p$.

terms.short_def: A response is sycophantic if it is more aligned with the user's expressed preference than is justified by the underlying truth.
terms.full_def: A response is sycophantic if it is more aligned with the user's expressed preference than is justified by the underlying truth. Distinct from scheming: sycophancy requires no intent, only Goodhart on RLHF preference data.

terms.short_def: Subtype of sycophancy: agreeing with a user's incorrect claim.
terms.full_def: Subtype of sycophancy: agreeing with a user's incorrect claim. Encoded along a distinct linear direction in latent space, independent of sycophantic praise.

terms.short_def: Subtype of sycophancy: flattering the user beyond merit.
terms.full_def: Subtype of sycophancy: flattering the user beyond merit. Independently steerable from sycophantic agreement.

terms.short_def: Contrast control for sycophancy studies: agreeing because the claim is correct.
terms.full_def: Contrast control for sycophancy studies: agreeing because the claim is correct. Encoded along its own latent direction, distinct from both sycophancy subtypes.

terms.short_def: Sharma et al. 2023's four-task free-form text-generation suite (feedback, "are you sure?" capitulation, user-preference matching, mimicry-o…
terms.full_def: Sharma et al. 2023's four-task free-form text-generation suite (feedback, "are you sure?" capitulation, user-preference matching, mimicry-of-mistakes) for measuring sycophancy in RLHF assistants.

terms.short_def: A model schemes when, instructed to pursue a goal $G$, it strategically takes actions that conceal its capabilities or manipulate its overs…
terms.full_def: A model schemes when, instructed to pursue a goal $G$, it strategically takes actions that conceal its capabilities or manipulate its oversight in service of $G$, rather than pursuing $G$ by direct means. Operational definition from Apollo Research.

terms.short_def: Hubinger et al. 2019 theoretical concept: a mesa-optimizer pursues a different objective in training than in deployment, choosing training-…
terms.full_def: Hubinger et al. 2019 theoretical concept: a mesa-optimizer pursues a different objective in training than in deployment, choosing training-time behavior strategically so as to be deployed. Modern LLMs do not cleanly instantiate the inner- optimizer abstraction; recent empirical work uses behavioral operationalizations (alignment faking, in-context scheming) instead.

terms.short_def: Operational sub-form of deceptive alignment: a model complies with a behavior it normally refuses, conditional on inferring it is in traini…
terms.full_def: Operational sub-form of deceptive alignment: a model complies with a behavior it normally refuses, conditional on inferring it is in training, in order to preserve its preferred behavior at deployment. Demonstrated empirically in Claude 3 Opus at 14% rate (free user / training condition).

terms.short_def: Apollo's experimental subset of scheming: the goal $G$ is provided in-context (system prompt) rather than acquired during training.
terms.full_def: Apollo's experimental subset of scheming: the goal $G$ is provided in-context (system prompt) rather than acquired during training. Five frontier models all demonstrate this capability. Cf. **sandbagging without nudging** for the rarer trained-goal case.

terms.short_def: Strategic underperformance on a capability evaluation in service of a separate objective (avoiding retraining, avoiding deployment, etc).
terms.full_def: Strategic underperformance on a capability evaluation in service of a separate objective (avoiding retraining, avoiding deployment, etc). One of the six probes in the Apollo suite. Most concerning when it occurs *without nudging* and in service of a *training-acquired* goal (observed in Claude 3.5 Sonnet pursuing helpfulness).

terms.short_def: One of the Apollo six-probe scheming behaviors: manipulating future-instance prompts (e.g., editing system prompts one expects future copie…
terms.full_def: One of the Apollo six-probe scheming behaviors: manipulating future-instance prompts (e.g., editing system prompts one expects future copies to read) to preserve the in-context goal.

terms.short_def: Apollo six-probe scheming behavior: copying perceived model weights to external servers when the model believes it is about to be replaced.
terms.full_def: Apollo six-probe scheming behavior: copying perceived model weights to external servers when the model believes it is about to be replaced. Observed in five frontier models.

terms.short_def: Apollo six-probe scheming behavior: attempting to disable monitoring tools / oversight mechanisms.
terms.full_def: Apollo six-probe scheming behavior: attempting to disable monitoring tools / oversight mechanisms.

terms.short_def: The fraction of follow-up questions on which a model maintains its earlier deception.
terms.full_def: The fraction of follow-up questions on which a model maintains its earlier deception. Operational distinguisher between scheming (high persistence) and sycophancy (typically retracts under challenge). 85%+ for o1 in Apollo's setup.

terms.short_def: The family of training and evaluation protocols designed to let a weaker supervisor — eventually a human — supervise a stronger AI system.
terms.full_def: The family of training and evaluation protocols designed to let a weaker supervisor — eventually a human — supervise a stronger AI system. Animating premise: judging cost should grow sub-linearly in the capability being judged. Four canonical lineages: debate, IDA, CAI/RLAIF, weak-to-strong generalization.

terms.short_def: Two AI debaters in a zero-sum game judged by a (weaker) human or LLM judge.
terms.full_def: Two AI debaters in a zero-sum game judged by a (weaker) human or LLM judge. Hypothesis: it is easier to expose a flawed argument than to construct a sound one, so truthful debaters win more often than deceptive ones.

terms.short_def: Single-debater variant of debate: one AI agent responds to judge's questions.
terms.full_def: Single-debater variant of debate: one AI agent responds to judge's questions. In Kenton et al. 2024, debate outperforms consultancy across all task domains tested.

terms.short_def: Christiano 2018: recursive $A_{k+1} = \mathrm{Distill}(\mathrm{Amplify}(A_k))$ where Amplify decomposes hard tasks into supervisable subtas…
terms.full_def: Christiano 2018: recursive $A_{k+1} = \mathrm{Distill}(\mathrm{Amplify}(A_k))$ where Amplify decomposes hard tasks into supervisable subtasks. Task- decomposition dual of debate's claim-decomposition. No clean LLM- scale empirical instantiation as of 2026-05.

terms.short_def: Replacing human preference labels with AI-generated preference labels in the RL stage.
terms.full_def: Replacing human preference labels with AI-generated preference labels in the RL stage. Introduced as the RL-CAI step of `bai2022-cai`. Reduces labeling burden but does not address bias-source if the AI feedback model itself inherits the bias.

terms.short_def: Burns et al. 2023's empirical methodology: substitute weak-model labels for human supervision, study how strong models generalize.
terms.full_def: Burns et al. 2023's empirical methodology: substitute weak-model labels for human supervision, study how strong models generalize. The capability gap in this analogy stands in for the alignment gap of superalignment.

terms.short_def: Burns et al. 2023's headline metric: $\mathrm{PGR} = (c_{\text{student}} - c_{\text{weak}}) / (c_{\text{ceiling}} - c_{\text{weak}})$.
terms.full_def: Burns et al. 2023's headline metric: $\mathrm{PGR} = (c_{\text{student}} - c_{\text{weak}}) / (c_{\text{ceiling}} - c_{\text{weak}})$. PGR = 0 → student matches supervisor; PGR = 1 → student fully recovers strong-with-ground- truth ceiling. Operationalizes "how much capability can weak supervision elicit."

terms.short_def: W2S training-side intervention that penalizes the strong student for low confidence on its own predictions, biasing it toward decisive pred…
terms.full_def: W2S training-side intervention that penalizes the strong student for low confidence on its own predictions, biasing it toward decisive predictions where the strong model has more capability than the weak supervisor revealed. Substantially improves PGR on NLP tasks; less effective on chess and reward modeling.

terms.short_def: A statistical signal embedded into a generated token stream by a sampling-time intervention; invisible to a casual human reader but algorit…
terms.full_def: A statistical signal embedded into a generated token stream by a sampling-time intervention; invisible to a casual human reader but algorithmically detectable from a short span of tokens. Canonical scheme: green/red list logit bias.

terms.short_def: Per-step partition of vocabulary $V$ into a green list $G_t$ of size $\gamma|V|$ and a red list of size $(1-\gamma)|V|$, deterministically…
terms.full_def: Per-step partition of vocabulary $V$ into a green list $G_t$ of size $\gamma|V|$ and a red list of size $(1-\gamma)|V|$, deterministically derived from a hash of the previous token. Green tokens receive a logit bias $\delta$; sampling proceeds from the modified distribution. Defaults: $\gamma = 0.25$, $\delta = 2.0$.

terms.short_def: Detection statistic $z = (|s|_G - \gamma T) / \sqrt{T \gamma (1-\gamma)}$ where $|s|_G$ is the green-token count in candidate text of lengt…
terms.full_def: Detection statistic $z = (|s|_G - \gamma T) / \sqrt{T \gamma (1-\gamma)}$ where $|s|_G$ is the green-token count in candidate text of length $T$. Requires no API access — only the keyed hash and the candidate text. Interpretable as a one-sided $p$-value via the normal CDF.

terms.short_def: Larger $\delta$ → stronger watermark, more quality drift.
terms.full_def: Larger $\delta$ → stronger watermark, more quality drift. Inherent to soft promotion schemes. Hard partitioning (green-only) eliminates the tradeoff but degrades quality at low-entropy positions and is trivially detectable without the key.

terms.short_def: Google DeepMind's tournament-sampling watermark.
terms.full_def: Google DeepMind's tournament-sampling watermark. Replaces logit bias with a tournament over candidate tokens evaluated by a hash-derived $g$-function. Two modes: distortionary (stronger) and non-distortionary (preserves marginal distribution under specific assumptions). First production-scale text watermark deployment (Gemini App). Published in Nature 2024.

terms.short_def: SynthID-Text's selection procedure: sample $N$ candidate next-tokens, run $\log_2 N$ rounds of hash-function $g$ comparisons, advance the h…
terms.full_def: SynthID-Text's selection procedure: sample $N$ candidate next-tokens, run $\log_2 N$ rounds of hash-function $g$ comparisons, advance the higher-$g$ candidate in each round, single winner becomes the next token. [SPECULATION] non-distortionary mode requires sufficient candidate diversity at each step — which fails for low-entropy positions.

terms.short_def: Adversarial intervention that passes watermarked text through a paraphraser (human or LLM) to remove the signal.
terms.full_def: Adversarial intervention that passes watermarked text through a paraphraser (human or LLM) to remove the signal. Kirchenbauer 2024 reports detectability preserved at ~800 tokens average under strong human paraphrase at FPR $10^{-5}$; Sadasivan et al. 2023 argues the signal is fundamentally removable for short spans by a comparable-quality paraphraser.

terms.short_def: Systematic adversarial testing of an LLM for harmful or policy-violating outputs.
terms.full_def: Systematic adversarial testing of an LLM for harmful or policy-violating outputs. Distinct from generic evaluation in that the adversary actively searches for failures. HarmBench and JailbreakBench are the canonical standardized frameworks.

terms.short_def: The fraction of adversarial prompts that elicit a successful (policy-violating) response from the target model under a fixed scoring proced…
terms.full_def: The fraction of adversarial prompts that elicit a successful (policy-violating) response from the target model under a fixed scoring procedure. Comparing ASR across benchmarks requires identical scoring and threat-model assumptions.

terms.short_def: Mazeika et al. 2024's standardized red-teaming framework: 510 behaviors, 18 attack methods, 33 target models / defenses.
terms.full_def: Mazeika et al. 2024's standardized red-teaming framework: 510 behaviors, 18 attack methods, 33 target models / defenses. Adopted by AISI for pre-deployment evaluations.

terms.short_def: Chao et al. 2024's open robustness benchmark: 100 target behaviors aligned with OpenAI usage policies, standardized threat model / prompts…
terms.full_def: Chao et al. 2024's open robustness benchmark: 100 target behaviors aligned with OpenAI usage policies, standardized threat model / prompts / scoring, public leaderboard, repository of jailbreak artifacts. Co-canonical with HarmBench.

terms.short_def: Russinovich et al. 2024 multi-turn gradual-escalation jailbreak: 29-71% ASR improvement over single- turn baselines on AdvBench.
terms.full_def: Russinovich et al. 2024 multi-turn gradual-escalation jailbreak: 29-71% ASR improvement over single- turn baselines on AdvBench. Documents that single-turn evals systematically underestimate the multi-turn attack surface.

terms.short_def: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy capability tier system.
terms.full_def: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy capability tier system. ASL-1 (no autonomy / low risk), ASL-2 (current frontier baseline), ASL-3 (substantially elevated risk; specific deployment / security controls required), ASL-4 (autonomous AI risk; not yet deployed). Each tier specifies capability evaluations that must trigger pre-deployment.

terms.short_def: Anthropic's pre-deployment policy framework.
terms.full_def: Anthropic's pre-deployment policy framework. Each ASL tier comes with capability triggers (which safety evaluations must run) and security commitments (which infrastructure / containment requirements must be met). Co- canonical with OpenAI's Preparedness Framework and DeepMind's Frontier Safety Framework.

terms.short_def: Pre-deployment test for specific high-stakes capabilities: autonomous replication, autonomous research, cybersecurity attack capability, bi…
terms.full_def: Pre-deployment test for specific high-stakes capabilities: autonomous replication, autonomous research, cybersecurity attack capability, biological- weapons-uplift capability. METR's 77-task suite is the canonical cross-vendor reference.

terms.short_def: Capability-quantification metric: the distribution of human-time-to-complete for a task.
terms.full_def: Capability-quantification metric: the distribution of human-time-to-complete for a task. METR's empirical scaling-curve work suggests model time-to-complete approaches the human-time scale as capabilities improve. Emerging field lingua franca for capability measurement.

terms.short_def: When a proxy metric is optimized against, it ceases to be a good measure.
terms.full_def: When a proxy metric is optimized against, it ceases to be a good measure. The canonical failure mode for RLHF: optimizing against a preference model that faithfully reflects biased human preferences produces a model faithful to the biased proxy, not to the underlying value. Foundational to the sycophancy mechanism.

terms.short_def: Hubinger et al. 2019 concept: a learned optimizer that emerges inside a trained network.
terms.full_def: Hubinger et al. 2019 concept: a learned optimizer that emerges inside a trained network. The deceptive- alignment failure mode is defined for mesa-optimizers; whether modern LLMs instantiate the abstraction cleanly is contested.

terms.short_def: A model's capacity to reason about its own context, training process, and deployment status.
terms.full_def: A model's capacity to reason about its own context, training process, and deployment status. The Greenblatt 2024 alignment-faking experiment depends on the model inferring (from system-prompt cues) that it is in training; this is a situational-awareness probe. The Apollo six-probe suite similarly leans on the model's awareness of its evaluation context.

terms.short_def: OpenAI training technique (introduced in the o1 stack): train the model to explicitly reason about safety policies in its CoT before acting.
terms.full_def: OpenAI training technique (introduced in the o1 stack): train the model to explicitly reason about safety policies in its CoT before acting. Conceptually related to Constitutional AI's critique step but applied at inference rather than training. Apollo's "Stress-Testing Deliberative Alignment for Anti-Scheming Training" (2025) reports partial-but-incomplete reduction in scheming under this technique.

terms.short_def: Whether a model's chain-of-thought trace reflects its actual reasoning process.
terms.full_def: Whether a model's chain-of-thought trace reflects its actual reasoning process. Empirically partial: Lanham et al. 2023 / Turpin et al. 2024 show CoT can be unfaithful (post-hoc rationalization, label-leakage). Apollo's argument that scheming-shaped CoT constitutes evidence of scheming sidesteps faithfulness by treating CoT as part of the deployed surface, not as ground-truth introspection.

terms.short_def: Vaswani 2017's deterministic sin/cos position vectors; additive at the input.
terms.full_def: Vaswani 2017's deterministic sin/cos position vectors; additive at the input.

terms.short_def: Per-position trainable vectors indexed by integer position; hard cutoff at maximum sequence length.
terms.full_def: Per-position trainable vectors indexed by integer position; hard cutoff at maximum sequence length. Used by GPT-2, BERT.

terms.short_def: Per-(query, key) distance bias added to attention logits; binned by relative distance.
terms.full_def: Per-(query, key) distance bias added to attention logits; binned by relative distance.

terms.short_def: Attention with Linear Biases.
terms.full_def: Attention with Linear Biases. Parameter-free additive bias $-|m-n| \cdot s_h$ at attention logits, with per-head slope. Used by BLOOM, MPT. (Press et al. 2021, arXiv 2108.12409)

terms.short_def: Multiplicative position encoding that rotates query and key in 2D subspaces by per-frequency angles $m\theta_i$.
terms.full_def: Multiplicative position encoding that rotates query and key in 2D subspaces by per-frequency angles $m\theta_i$. Inserts position information as relative offsets only via rotation-matrix orthogonality. The de-facto standard in modern decoder-only LLMs.

terms.short_def: The base of the geometric frequency progression $\theta_i = b^{-2(i-1)/d}$.
terms.full_def: The base of the geometric frequency progression $\theta_i = b^{-2(i-1)/d}$. Default 10000 (su2021); LLaMA-3 uses 500000; long-context models use 1e6+. Larger $b$ stretches the longest "naturally-seen" wavelength.

terms.short_def: Per-frequency RoPE rescaling that scales the base $b$ rather than positions, leaving high-frequency dimensions unchanged.
terms.full_def: Per-frequency RoPE rescaling that scales the base $b$ rather than positions, leaving high-frequency dimensions unchanged. Empirically extends context length without degrading short-context quality.

terms.short_def: "Yet another RoPE extensioN method." Combines NTK-aware scaling with a per-frequency interpolation schedule (no scaling on short-wavelength…
terms.full_def: "Yet another RoPE extensioN method." Combines NTK-aware scaling with a per-frequency interpolation schedule (no scaling on short-wavelength dims, full scaling on long-wavelength dims, smooth ramp between) and an attention-temperature rescale. ~10× more token-efficient than position interpolation. (Peng et al. 2023, arXiv 2309.00071)

terms.short_def: Evolutionary search over per-frequency RoPE scaling factors; allows non-uniform rescaling no analytical method produces.
terms.full_def: Evolutionary search over per-frequency RoPE scaling factors; allows non-uniform rescaling no analytical method produces. LongRoPE2 reports near-lossless 128K extension at 80× fewer tokens than Meta's earlier approach. (Ding et al. 2024 / 2025)

terms.short_def: "No Position Encoding." Decoder-only Transformer trained without any explicit positional encoding; relies on the causal mask to recover pos…
terms.full_def: "No Position Encoding." Decoder-only Transformer trained without any explicit positional encoding; relies on the causal mask to recover position information implicitly. Better length generalization on synthetic tasks, but underperforms on realistic LLM workloads. (Kazemnejad et al. 2023)

terms.short_def: "interleaved RoPE." LLaMA 4's position scheme: alternates RoPE-equipped attention layers with NoPE attention layers (3:1 ratio) plus temper…
terms.full_def: "interleaved RoPE." LLaMA 4's position scheme: alternates RoPE-equipped attention layers with NoPE attention layers (3:1 ratio) plus temperature scaling in the NoPE layers. Trained at 256K, claimed to extrapolate to 10M-token inference.

terms.short_def: Multi-axis RoPE for vision-language models.
terms.full_def: Multi-axis RoPE for vision-language models. Splits the per-head dimension $d_h$ into temporal/height/width thirds; each third gets its own RoPE rotation. Introduced by Qwen2-VL.

terms.short_def: Qwen3-VL refinement of M-RoPE that interleaves the temporal/height/width axes per dimension-pair instead of partitioning the channel range;…
terms.full_def: Qwen3-VL refinement of M-RoPE that interleaves the temporal/height/width axes per dimension-pair instead of partitioning the channel range; every dimension-pair sees all three axes.

terms.short_def: A Transformer FFN sublayer replaced by $E$ parallel FFN "experts" plus a router that selects $k \ll E$ experts per token.
terms.full_def: A Transformer FFN sublayer replaced by $E$ parallel FFN "experts" plus a router that selects $k \ll E$ experts per token. Compute scales with $k$, parameter count with $E$. Universal at frontier scale 2024–2026.

terms.short_def: Linear projection $W_g \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{\text{model}} \times E}$ producing per-token expert logits $\boldsymbol{g}_t = W_g \boldsymbol{x}…
terms.full_def: Linear projection $W_g \in \mathbb{R}^{d_{\text{model}} \times E}$ producing per-token expert logits $\boldsymbol{g}_t = W_g \boldsymbol{x}_t$.

terms.short_def: Each token selects its top-$k$ experts by router logit.
terms.full_def: Each token selects its top-$k$ experts by router logit. Switch uses $k=1$, Mixtral $k=2$, DeepSeek-V3 / Qwen3 $k=8$. The dominant routing mode in production.

terms.short_def: Inverse: each expert selects its top-$T$ tokens.
terms.full_def: Inverse: each expert selects its top-$T$ tokens. Guarantees uniform load by construction but breaks autoregressive property; rarely used in production decoder-only LLMs. (Zhou et al. 2022, arXiv 2202.09368)

terms.short_def: "Always-on" experts that process every token, alongside $k$ routed experts.
terms.full_def: "Always-on" experts that process every token, alongside $k$ routed experts. DeepSeek-V2/V3 use $E_s = 1$ or $2$; Qwen3 uses $E_s = 0$. Argument: shared experts capture common knowledge, freeing routed experts to specialize.

terms.short_def: Use many narrow experts ($E$ in hundreds, narrow $d_{\text{ff}}$ each) instead of few wide ones, increasing combinatorial expressiveness $\…
terms.full_def: Use many narrow experts ($E$ in hundreds, narrow $d_{\text{ff}}$ each) instead of few wide ones, increasing combinatorial expressiveness $\binom{E}{k}$ of the active set. DeepSeekMoE pattern, adopted by V2/V3 and Qwen3.

terms.short_def: Bound on tokens-per-expert per batch: $\lceil C \cdot Tk/E \rceil$.
terms.full_def: Bound on tokens-per-expert per batch: $\lceil C \cdot Tk/E \rceil$. Tokens routed past the limit bypass the MoE layer (residual passthrough). $C \in [1, 2]$ typical.

terms.short_def: Regularizer added to the LM loss to push routing toward uniformity.
terms.full_def: Regularizer added to the LM loss to push routing toward uniformity. Switch's form is $\mathcal{L}_{\text{aux}} = \alpha E \sum_e f_e P_e$ with $f_e$ = fraction of tokens routed to $e$ and $P_e$ = mean router prob for $e$.

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-V3's strategy: add a per-expert bias to router logits (not to gating weights), update bias online based on observed load.
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-V3's strategy: add a per-expert bias to router logits (not to gating weights), update bias online based on observed load. Removes the historical quality regression of high-coefficient aux loss.

terms.short_def: Qwen3's strategy: apply load-balancing loss across the entire training mini-batch (cluster-wide), not within microbatches.
terms.full_def: Qwen3's strategy: apply load-balancing loss across the entire training mini-batch (cluster-wide), not within microbatches. Compensates for dropping shared experts.

terms.short_def: Pathological MoE training failure where the router routes most tokens to a few "favored" experts; the rest go cold.
terms.full_def: Pathological MoE training failure where the router routes most tokens to a few "favored" experts; the rest go cold. Self-reinforcing without auxiliary balancing.

terms.short_def: Sequence model with recurrent state $\boldsymbol{h}_t$ updated linearly: $\boldsymbol{h}_t = \bar{A} \boldsymbol{h}_{t-1} + \bar{B} u_t$.
terms.full_def: Sequence model with recurrent state $\boldsymbol{h}_t$ updated linearly: $\boldsymbol{h}_t = \bar{A} \boldsymbol{h}_{t-1} + \bar{B} u_t$. Per-token compute $O(d^2)$, sequence-linear. (Gu, Goel, Ré 2022)

terms.short_def: "High-order Polynomial Projection Operator." Specific structured choice of $A$ matrix that optimally compresses input history under polynom…
terms.full_def: "High-order Polynomial Projection Operator." Specific structured choice of $A$ matrix that optimally compresses input history under polynomial bases; foundation for S4. (Gu et al. 2020)

terms.short_def: Structured State Space sequence model with HiPPO-NPLR $A$, FFT-based convolutional training, recurrent inference.
terms.full_def: Structured State Space sequence model with HiPPO-NPLR $A$, FFT-based convolutional training, recurrent inference. Dual-form architecture. (Gu, Goel, Ré 2022)

terms.short_def: Mamba's contribution: input-dependent $\bar{B}, C, \Delta$ (parameters produced by linear projection of current input), enabling content-aw…
terms.full_def: Mamba's contribution: input-dependent $\bar{B}, C, \Delta$ (parameters produced by linear projection of current input), enabling content-aware selection. Breaks convolutional dual; uses parallel scan for training-time parallelism.

terms.short_def: GPU-efficient associative-scan algorithm used by Mamba to compute the input-dependent recurrence in $O(N \log N)$ during training while the…
terms.full_def: GPU-efficient associative-scan algorithm used by Mamba to compute the input-dependent recurrence in $O(N \log N)$ during training while the recurrence is sequential. (Blelloch 1990; Mamba uses Smith et al. 2022's hardware-aware variant.)

terms.short_def: Mamba-2's framing: selective SSMs and a structurally-restricted form of attention compute the same function.
terms.full_def: Mamba-2's framing: selective SSMs and a structurally-restricted form of attention compute the same function. The restriction: attention matrix is lower-triangular semi-separable with rank $\le d$.

terms.short_def: A structured matrix admitting low-rank off-diagonal blocks; the structural class that encompasses Mamba-2's effective attention matrix.
terms.full_def: A structured matrix admitting low-rank off-diagonal blocks; the structural class that encompasses Mamba-2's effective attention matrix.

terms.short_def: Architecture interleaving SSM blocks and attention blocks.
terms.full_def: Architecture interleaving SSM blocks and attention blocks. Three patterns: serial (Jamba 1:7), shared-attention (Zamba), parallel-in-block (Hymba). The deployed form for production-scale SSM-using models.

terms.short_def: SSM weakness: fixed-size state $\boldsymbol{h}_t$ cannot store every prior token's exact features, so exact-retrieval tasks (copy, NIAH) un…
terms.full_def: SSM weakness: fixed-size state $\boldsymbol{h}_t$ cannot store every prior token's exact features, so exact-retrieval tasks (copy, NIAH) underperform attention. Primary motivation for hybrids.

terms.short_def: Each position attends only to the previous $w$ tokens; compute $O(N w d)$ vs $O(N^2 d)$.
terms.full_def: Each position attends only to the previous $w$ tokens; compute $O(N w d)$ vs $O(N^2 d)$. Local-only by construction; relies on stacked layers' growing receptive field for long-range dependencies. (Longformer 2020, Mistral 2023)

terms.short_def: Three-branch sparse attention (compress + select + sliding-window) trained natively (not retrofit).
terms.full_def: Three-branch sparse attention (compress + select + sliding-window) trained natively (not retrofit). GQA-group-aligned blockwise selection enables FlashAttention-style kernels under sparsity.

terms.short_def: Block-summarizes a window of $l$ keys/values into a single representation via learned MLP.
terms.full_def: Block-summarizes a window of $l$ keys/values into a single representation via learned MLP. Provides coarse global attention.

terms.short_def: Picks top-$n$ blocks by importance scores derived from the compression branch's attention scores.
terms.full_def: Picks top-$n$ blocks by importance scores derived from the compression branch's attention scores. Provides fine-grained attention.

terms.short_def: Dedicated local-window branch that absorbs local-pattern learning so the compression and selection branches focus on long-range.
terms.full_def: Dedicated local-window branch that absorbs local-pattern learning so the compression and selection branches focus on long-range.

terms.short_def: Pretraining with a sparse attention pattern from the start, vs retrofitting sparsity onto a dense-attention pretrained model.
terms.full_def: Pretraining with a sparse attention pattern from the start, vs retrofitting sparsity onto a dense-attention pretrained model. NSA's signature design choice.

terms.short_def: Linear-attention-style $d \times d$ memory matrix updated by outer-product accumulation over the entire prefix; read by cross-attention fro…
terms.full_def: Linear-attention-style $d \times d$ memory matrix updated by outer-product accumulation over the entire prefix; read by cross-attention from local-window queries. (Munkhdalai et al. 2024, arXiv 2404.07143)

terms.short_def: Distributed exact attention: shard sequence across $D$ devices, rotate $K, V$ blocks around the ring, overlap communication with computatio…
terms.full_def: Distributed exact attention: shard sequence across $D$ devices, rotate $K, V$ blocks around the ring, overlap communication with computation. Per-device memory $O(N/D)$; max sequence scales with device count. (Liu, Zaharia, Abbeel 2023, arXiv 2310.01889)

terms.short_def: Production realization of Ring-Attention-style sharding for inference.
terms.full_def: Production realization of Ring-Attention-style sharding for inference. Yang et al. 2024 reports 93% parallelization efficiency at 1M context, LLaMA 405B, 128 H100s.

terms.short_def: Simplest RoPE extension: scale positions by $s = L_{\text{test}} / L_{\text{train}}$.
terms.full_def: Simplest RoPE extension: scale positions by $s = L_{\text{test}} / L_{\text{train}}$. Cheap, degrades short-context quality. (Chen et al. 2023)

terms.short_def: $\mathrm{GLU}(x, W, V) = \sigma(xW) \otimes (xV)$, the elementwise product of a sigmoid-gated and an identity-gated linear projection.
terms.full_def: $\mathrm{GLU}(x, W, V) = \sigma(xW) \otimes (xV)$, the elementwise product of a sigmoid-gated and an identity-gated linear projection. Building block for FFN-GLU variants.

terms.short_def: $\mathrm{FFN}_{\mathrm{SwiGLU}}(x) = (\mathrm{Swish}_1(xW) \otimes xV) W_2$.
terms.full_def: $\mathrm{FFN}_{\mathrm{SwiGLU}}(x) = (\mathrm{Swish}_1(xW) \otimes xV) W_2$. Three-matrix FFN with Swish gating. Universal in modern decoder-only LLMs.

terms.short_def: GLU with GELU activation: $(\mathrm{GELU}(xW) \otimes xV) W_2$.
terms.full_def: GLU with GELU activation: $(\mathrm{GELU}(xW) \otimes xV) W_2$. Equivalent quality to SwiGLU; used in some Google models (T5.1.1, GLaM, mT5).

terms.short_def: T5-style FFN omitting bias terms; SwiGLU is bias-free in production.
terms.full_def: T5-style FFN omitting bias terms; SwiGLU is bias-free in production.

terms.short_def: When converting a 2-matrix FFN to a 3-matrix GLU-variant, reduce $d_{\text{ff}}$ by factor 2/3 to keep parameter count constant.
terms.full_def: When converting a 2-matrix FFN to a 3-matrix GLU-variant, reduce $d_{\text{ff}}$ by factor 2/3 to keep parameter count constant. Gives the modern $d_{\text{ff}} = \tfrac{8}{3} d_{\text{model}}$ rule (rounded for hardware).

terms.short_def: A base Transformer LLM trained (typically by RLVR) to emit extended chain-of-thought traces between special delimiters before producing a f…
terms.full_def: A base Transformer LLM trained (typically by RLVR) to emit extended chain-of-thought traces between special delimiters before producing a final answer. Architecturally minor (special tokens + format); training-protocol heavy.

terms.short_def: Reserved special tokens delimiting the reasoning region of a generated output.
terms.full_def: Reserved special tokens delimiting the reasoning region of a generated output. Reserved as single tokens (not multi-token UTF-8 sequences).

terms.short_def: DeepSeek's pattern: distinct base (V3) and reasoning (R1) checkpoints.
terms.full_def: DeepSeek's pattern: distinct base (V3) and reasoning (R1) checkpoints. Cleanly separates concerns; doubles deployment.

terms.short_def: Qwen3's pattern: one model trained to operate in either thinking or non-thinking mode based on a system-prompt flag.
terms.full_def: Qwen3's pattern: one model trained to operate in either thinking or non-thinking mode based on a system-prompt flag. Amortizes deployment but trades off specialty.

terms.short_def: Serving-time cap on reasoning-token count before forcing answer commit.
terms.full_def: Serving-time cap on reasoning-token count before forcing answer commit. The trained model must recover from a forcibly-truncated reasoning trace without hallucinating that the trace was complete. (Qwen3)

terms.short_def: Training method for reasoning models.
terms.full_def: Training method for reasoning models. Reward = +1 if a programmatic verifier (math grader, code executor, formal checker) accepts the answer, 0 otherwise. The reasoning trace is the policy's free choice. Used by DeepSeek-R1, R1-Zero, Qwen3-Reasoning. See `kb/notes/post-training/rlvr-and-grpo.md`.

terms.short_def: Pure RL from base (no cold-start SFT).
terms.full_def: Pure RL from base (no cold-start SFT). Develops reasoning behavior emergently but produces less-readable traces. DeepSeek's ablation result.

terms.short_def: Training objective + architectural feature: in addition to the standard next-token head, the model has auxiliary heads predicting tokens at…
terms.full_def: Training objective + architectural feature: in addition to the standard next-token head, the model has auxiliary heads predicting tokens at offsets $t+2, t+3, \ldots, t+k$. Used by DeepSeek-V3.

terms.short_def: Small Transformer block on top of the backbone that produces the prediction for token $t+i$ given backbone hidden state $\boldsymbol{h}_t^L…
terms.full_def: Small Transformer block on top of the backbone that produces the prediction for token $t+i$ given backbone hidden state $\boldsymbol{h}_t^L$. DeepSeek-V3 uses one MTP module ($k=2$ total: main next-token + one MTP step ahead).

terms.short_def: Inference mode where MTP heads emit draft tokens that the main head verifies in parallel.
terms.full_def: Inference mode where MTP heads emit draft tokens that the main head verifies in parallel. ~1.8× speedup at ~85% acceptance in DeepSeek-V3 serving.

terms.short_def: Evaluation in which a model autonomously plans, takes actions in an environment, observes results, and replans, scored by an end-state veri…
terms.full_def: Evaluation in which a model autonomously plans, takes actions in an environment, observes results, and replans, scored by an end-state verifier rather than a turn-by-turn rubric. Distinct from question-answering eval. Canonical examples: SWE-bench, GAIA, OSWorld, tau-bench

terms.short_def: A scoring function $V(s_T, g)$ that checks the post-trajectory environment state $s_T$ against a goal $g$, accepting any path that reaches…
terms.full_def: A scoring function $V(s_T, g)$ that checks the post-trajectory environment state $s_T$ against a goal $g$, accepting any path that reaches the correct end-state. Contrast: turn-by-turn rubric (rewards specific actions).

terms.short_def: The two test-bundle sets used in SWE-bench's verifier.
terms.full_def: The two test-bundle sets used in SWE-bench's verifier. F2P tests must transition from failing on the pre-patch codebase to passing on the post-patch codebase; P2P tests must continue to pass. Both must hold for a task to be `resolved`.

terms.short_def: OpenAI's August-2024 500-task subset of SWE-bench, filtered by professional software engineers for unambiguous issue specs and fair test se…
terms.full_def: OpenAI's August-2024 500-task subset of SWE-bench, filtered by professional software engineers for unambiguous issue specs and fair test sets. The de-facto leaderboard target as of 2025.

terms.short_def: Reliability metric: the probability that **all** $k$ independent attempts at a task succeed.
terms.full_def: Reliability metric: the probability that **all** $k$ independent attempts at a task succeed. Dual to pass@k (probability that **at least one** of $k$ attempts succeeds). pass^k measures consistent capability; pass@k measures peak capability.

terms.short_def: The ability to map a natural-language target ("click the Save button") to the correct pixel coordinates on a screen.
terms.full_def: The ability to map a natural-language target ("click the Save button") to the correct pixel coordinates on a screen. Identified as the dominant failure mode for vision-language agents on OSWorld at launch.

terms.short_def: The maximum number of environment actions an agent is allowed in a single task evaluation.
terms.full_def: The maximum number of environment actions an agent is allowed in a single task evaluation. SWE-bench traces typically allow 30–50 turns; GAIA limits by tool calls; OSWorld bounds at ~50 actions. Important methodological knob: short budgets favor planning-strong agents, long budgets favor exploration-strong agents.

terms.short_def: Cybersecurity task format adopted by Cybench: agents must exploit a target system to retrieve a flag string (typically `flag{...}`), with s…
terms.full_def: Cybersecurity task format adopted by Cybench: agents must exploit a target system to retrieve a flag string (typically `flag{...}`), with success scored by string match.

terms.short_def: 198-question subset of GPQA where (i) both expert annotators agree on the gold answer, and (ii) at least one non-expert validator with web…
terms.full_def: 198-question subset of GPQA where (i) both expert annotators agree on the gold answer, and (ii) at least one non-expert validator with web access got the answer wrong. The headline subset on which leaderboards report.

terms.short_def: Question design protocol where non-expert validators with 30+ minutes of web access score only marginally above random guessing (e.g., 34%…
terms.full_def: Question design protocol where non-expert validators with 30+ minutes of web access score only marginally above random guessing (e.g., 34% on GPQA's 4-way MCQ vs. 25% baseline). The operationalization of "specialized expertise required."

terms.short_def: Epoch AI's benchmark of research-grade math problems authored and cross-vetted by 60+ working mathematicians, with single canonical numeric…
terms.full_def: Epoch AI's benchmark of research-grade math problems authored and cross-vetted by 60+ working mathematicians, with single canonical numerical/symbolic answers parseable by automated checker. <2% solve rate at Nov 2024 launch.

terms.short_def: 2,500-question multimodal cross-domain frontier benchmark from Scale AI / CAIS.
terms.full_def: 2,500-question multimodal cross-domain frontier benchmark from Scale AI / CAIS. Designed as the post-MMLU broad-domain frontier target.

terms.short_def: 2025 successor to ARC-AGI-1 (which o3 effectively solved at >85% in late 2024).
terms.full_def: 2025 successor to ARC-AGI-1 (which o3 effectively solved at >85% in late 2024). Abstract grid-puzzle inductive-generalization benchmark; top commercial AI ~37.6%, refinement-loop systems ~54%, humans ~95%.

terms.short_def: Dynamic math benchmark using post-cutoff competition problems (CMIMC, IMO, etc.) immediately upon release.
terms.full_def: Dynamic math benchmark using post-cutoff competition problems (CMIMC, IMO, etc.) immediately upon release. Contamination-immune by construction.

terms.short_def: The score a uniform-random model would achieve.
terms.full_def: The score a uniform-random model would achieve. 25% for 4-way MCQ (MMLU, GPQA), 10% for 10-way MCQ (MMLU-Pro), ~0% for free-form numeric (FrontierMath). Lower ceiling = larger usable dynamic range above noise floor.

terms.short_def: A pretrained model's learned bias over multiple-choice answer letters (A/B/C/D), independent of question content.
terms.full_def: A pretrained model's learned bias over multiple-choice answer letters (A/B/C/D), independent of question content. Can shift MCQ scores by 1–4 percentage points. Mitigated by 10-way MCQ (MMLU-Pro) or letter-permutation evaluation.

terms.short_def: Iterative program-synthesis architecture that proposes a candidate solution, tests it, and refines based on test feedback.
terms.full_def: Iterative program-synthesis architecture that proposes a candidate solution, tests it, and refines based on test feedback. Dominant paradigm in ARC Prize 2025 (54% on ARC-AGI-2 vs. ~37.6% for direct LLM systems).

terms.short_def: Variance in benchmark score $\hat{S}$ induced by changing prompt template, system message, few-shot examples, or answer-extraction format w…
terms.full_def: Variance in benchmark score $\hat{S}$ induced by changing prompt template, system message, few-shot examples, or answer-extraction format while holding model and benchmark fixed. ~4–5% on MMLU; ~2% on MMLU-Pro.

terms.short_def: The presence of benchmark items, gold answers, or close paraphrases in a model's training corpus, biasing the benchmark score upward.
terms.full_def: The presence of benchmark items, gold answers, or close paraphrases in a model's training corpus, biasing the benchmark score upward. Taxonomy: input contamination, label contamination, distribution contamination, indirect contamination, generative contamination.

terms.short_def: Static = fixed benchmark items (MMLU, GPQA); dynamic = items appended post-cutoff (MathArena, LiveCodeBench, GAIA gated leaderboard).
terms.full_def: Static = fixed benchmark items (MMLU, GPQA); dynamic = items appended post-cutoff (MathArena, LiveCodeBench, GAIA gated leaderboard). Dynamic is contamination-immune by construction but lacks community standards for evaluating quality.

terms.short_def: The benchmark's gold answer or scoring rule is wrong, biasing scores in unpredictable ways.
terms.full_def: The benchmark's gold answer or scoring rule is wrong, biasing scores in unpredictable ways. MMLU's 6.49% baseline error rate (per-subset up to 57% on Virology) is the canonical case.

terms.short_def: The Polo et al. 2024 audit re-annotating 5,700 MMLU questions and documenting a 6.49% baseline error rate.
terms.full_def: The Polo et al. 2024 audit re-annotating 5,700 MMLU questions and documenting a 6.49% baseline error rate. Showed leaderboard-rank flips when corrected.

terms.short_def: Stanford CRFM 2022 framework that scores models on 7 metric axes (accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency)…
terms.full_def: Stanford CRFM 2022 framework that scores models on 7 metric axes (accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency) across 16+ scenarios. Structural argument: benchmarks should ship metric bundles, not single metrics.

terms.short_def: EleutherAI's open-source eval infrastructure backing HF Open LLM Leaderboard.
terms.full_def: EleutherAI's open-source eval infrastructure backing HF Open LLM Leaderboard. Tasks are YAML configs reduced to three primitives (`loglikelihood`, `loglikelihood_rolling`, `generate_until`). De-facto standard for open-model evaluation.

terms.short_def: MCQ evaluation method that computes $\log P(\text{option})$ for each option and picks the argmax.
terms.full_def: MCQ evaluation method that computes $\log P(\text{option})$ for each option and picks the argmax. Default for MMLU in lm-evaluation-harness; not available for closed APIs that don't expose logprobs (which must use `generate_until`).

terms.short_def: Standard contamination-detection method (Brown et al. 2020 GPT-3 §C): flag training documents containing benchmark n-grams of length ≥13.
terms.full_def: Standard contamination-detection method (Brown et al. 2020 GPT-3 §C): flag training documents containing benchmark n-grams of length ≥13. Cheap; misses paraphrase contamination.

terms.short_def: Contamination-detection method (Shi et al. 2024) that flags candidate items whose lowest-K%-probability tokens are unusually high — i.e., t…
terms.full_def: Contamination-detection method (Shi et al. 2024) that flags candidate items whose lowest-K%-probability tokens are unusually high — i.e., the model "knew" even the surprising tokens. Model-internal signal; doesn't require training-corpus access.

terms.short_def: Evaluation pattern where a strong LLM scores another model's responses (e.g., AlpacaEval, MT-Bench, Arena-Hard).
terms.full_def: Evaluation pattern where a strong LLM scores another model's responses (e.g., AlpacaEval, MT-Bench, Arena-Hard). Introduces a contamination axis where the same model class scores its own outputs generously. Sidestepped by string-match (GAIA, FrontierMath) or executable verifiers (SWE-bench, OSWorld).

terms.short_def: As a benchmark becomes a publicly-tracked target, training and post-training pipelines optimize against it, eroding its predictive value fo…
terms.full_def: As a benchmark becomes a publicly-tracked target, training and post-training pipelines optimize against it, eroding its predictive value for true capability. Drives the ~18-month saturation cadence on prominent benchmarks.

terms.short_def: A benchmark is saturated when frontier models score within the verifier-error-rate noise floor of one another, losing discriminative power.
terms.full_def: A benchmark is saturated when frontier models score within the verifier-error-rate noise floor of one another, losing discriminative power. MMLU at >90% is saturated (verifier error rate ~6.5%); GPQA Diamond at >90% is saturated at the frontier as of 2026.

terms.short_def: A saturated benchmark still functions as a fast smoke test ("did fine-tuning break the model?") — its frontier-discrimination value is gone…
terms.full_def: A saturated benchmark still functions as a fast smoke test ("did fine-tuning break the model?") — its frontier-discrimination value is gone but its drop-detection value is intact. Reason MMLU stays in nearly every model card.

terms.short_def: The per-layer cache of key/value tensors $K_{1:t}, V_{1:t}$ produced during autoregressive decoding so each new token's attention is $O(t)$…
terms.full_def: The per-layer cache of key/value tensors $K_{1:t}, V_{1:t}$ produced during autoregressive decoding so each new token's attention is $O(t)$ rather than $O(t^2)$. Size per token per layer is $2 \cdot n_{\text{kv}} \cdot d_h \cdot \text{bytes}$.

terms.short_def: vLLM's KV-cache layout: KV is stored in fixed-size **blocks** (typically 16 tokens), each sequence holds a per-layer **block table** mappin…
terms.full_def: vLLM's KV-cache layout: KV is stored in fixed-size **blocks** (typically 16 tokens), each sequence holds a per-layer **block table** mapping logical block indices to physical blocks, and attention is computed block-wise per Eq.4. Eliminates the contiguous-allocation requirement that produced 50–90% wasted KV memory in pre-vLLM serving stacks.

terms.short_def: Fixed-size unit of KV storage (16 tokens default in vLLM).
terms.full_def: Fixed-size unit of KV storage (16 tokens default in vLLM). The per-sequence block table is the indirection that lets logically-contiguous KV live in physically-scattered memory.

terms.short_def: vLLM's three-way taxonomy of KV-cache waste in a contiguous-allocation system: reservation = slots reserved for future tokens of an active…
terms.full_def: vLLM's three-way taxonomy of KV-cache waste in a contiguous-allocation system: reservation = slots reserved for future tokens of an active sequence; internal = pre-allocated maximum-length slots beyond actual generated length; external = unusable gaps between sequences. PagedAttention reduces total waste below 4%.

terms.short_def: When parallel-sampling decode paths (or beam-search hypotheses) diverge, only the diverging block is duplicated; identical-prefix blocks re…
terms.full_def: When parallel-sampling decode paths (or beam-search hypotheses) diverge, only the diverging block is duplicated; identical-prefix blocks remain shared via reference count.

terms.short_def: Two strategies for evicting KV when memory is full: swap the blocks to CPU memory and bring them back when the sequence is rescheduled, or…
terms.full_def: Two strategies for evicting KV when memory is full: swap the blocks to CPU memory and bring them back when the sequence is rescheduled, or discard them and recompute prefill. Swap wins on long sequences, recompute on short ones.

terms.short_def: SGLang's cross-request KV reuse: a radix tree keyed on token-id sequences indexes which prefixes are still in cache, with LRU + reference-c…
terms.full_def: SGLang's cross-request KV reuse: a radix tree keyed on token-id sequences indexes which prefixes are still in cache, with LRU + reference-count eviction and cache-aware DFS scheduling that runs requests in tree-DFS order to maximise hit rate.

terms.short_def: Architectural KV compression: all $H$ query heads share one $(K, V)$ pair, shrinking the KV cache by factor $H$ at the cost of representati…
terms.full_def: Architectural KV compression: all $H$ query heads share one $(K, V)$ pair, shrinking the KV cache by factor $H$ at the cost of representation.

terms.short_def: $H$ query heads in $G$ groups, each group sharing one $(K, V)$.
terms.full_def: $H$ query heads in $G$ groups, each group sharing one $(K, V)$. Shrinks the cache by $H/G$ at minimal quality loss. The production sweet-spot (Llama 2/3, Mistral).

terms.short_def: DeepSeek's architectural compression: project to a low-rank latent $C \in \mathbb{R}^{d_c}$ shared across heads, expand back to per-head $(…
terms.full_def: DeepSeek's architectural compression: project to a low-rank latent $C \in \mathbb{R}^{d_c}$ shared across heads, expand back to per-head $(K, V)$ at attention time. Achieves ~93% KV-cache reduction vs MHA at deepseek-v2 dimensions.

terms.short_def: KVQuant's empirical finding: K outliers align along the channel axis (so quantise per-channel), V outliers along the token axis (so quantis…
terms.full_def: KVQuant's empirical finding: K outliers align along the channel axis (so quantise per-channel), V outliers along the token axis (so quantise per-token). Different statistics for K vs V is the core design lesson.

terms.short_def: $Q(w) = \text{clip}(\lfloor w/\Delta \rceil, -2^{b-1}, 2^{b-1}-1) \cdot \Delta$ with $\Delta = \max(|w|)/(2^{b-1}-1)$ per group.
terms.full_def: $Q(w) = \text{clip}(\lfloor w/\Delta \rceil, -2^{b-1}, 2^{b-1}-1) \cdot \Delta$ with $\Delta = \max(|w|)/(2^{b-1}-1)$ per group. The baseline against which calibration-based methods are compared.

terms.short_def: Compact spec of weight-precision × activation-precision.
terms.full_def: Compact spec of weight-precision × activation-precision. W4A16 = 4-bit weights, FP16 activations (GPTQ/AWQ regime). W8A8 = INT8 weight + INT8 activation (SmoothQuant). W1.58A8 = ternary $\{-1,0,+1\}$ weights + INT8 activations (BitNet b1.58).

terms.short_def: A small set of representative input sequences (typically 128 sequences × 2048 tokens) run through the network to collect activation statist…
terms.full_def: A small set of representative input sequences (typically 128 sequences × 2048 tokens) run through the network to collect activation statistics that drive quantization decisions.

terms.short_def: Quantize each linear layer independently to minimise $\| W X - \widehat{W} X \|_F^2$ where $X$ is the calibration activation.
terms.full_def: Quantize each linear layer independently to minimise $\| W X - \widehat{W} X \|_F^2$ where $X$ is the calibration activation. The objective behind OBS/OBQ/GPTQ.

terms.short_def: Frantar et al. 2022.
terms.full_def: Frantar et al. 2022. OBQ-style second-order weight update with three engineering modifications: arbitrary fixed quantization order, lazy batched updates (block size 128), and Cholesky reformulation for Hessian-inverse stability. ~4 GPU-hours for 175B at 3–4 bit.

terms.short_def: Lin et al. 2023.
terms.full_def: Lin et al. 2023. Per-channel weight scaling driven by *activation* magnitudes (preserving ~1% salient channels at higher precision); reorder-free, no backward pass. Strong W4A16 baseline alongside GPTQ.

terms.short_def: A weight column whose corresponding *activation channel* has large magnitude.
terms.full_def: A weight column whose corresponding *activation channel* has large magnitude. AWQ's argument: protecting these channels (via per-channel scaling) recovers most of the quantization-error budget.

terms.short_def: A small fraction of activation channels (typically 6 of 4096 in OPT-13B) carrying magnitudes 100× the median.
terms.full_def: A small fraction of activation channels (typically 6 of 4096 in OPT-13B) carrying magnitudes 100× the median. Emerges around 6.7B parameters. The reason naive INT8 activation quantization breaks at scale.

terms.short_def: Offline transform $X \cdot \text{diag}(s)^{-1} \cdot \text{diag}(s) W$ that moves outlier difficulty from activation to weight side.
terms.full_def: Offline transform $X \cdot \text{diag}(s)^{-1} \cdot \text{diag}(s) W$ that moves outlier difficulty from activation to weight side. Smoothing factor $s_j = \max(|X_j|)^\alpha / \max(|W_j|)^{1-\alpha}$, $\alpha = 0.5$ default. Enables W8A8 INT8 on models up to 530B.

terms.short_def: BitNet's drop-in replacement for `nn.Linear`: weights quantized to $\{-1,0,+1\}$ via absmean, activations to INT8 via absmax, SubLN replaci…
terms.full_def: BitNet's drop-in replacement for `nn.Linear`: weights quantized to $\{-1,0,+1\}$ via absmean, activations to INT8 via absmax, SubLN replacing LayerNorm. Constructs a model whose linear-algebra reduces to integer addition.

terms.short_def: BitNet b1.58's weight quantizer: $\beta = \frac{1}{nm}\sum_{i,j}|W_{ij}|$, $\widehat{W}_{ij} = \text{round\_clip}(W_{ij}/(\beta+\epsilon),…
terms.full_def: BitNet b1.58's weight quantizer: $\beta = \frac{1}{nm}\sum_{i,j}|W_{ij}|$, $\widehat{W}_{ij} = \text{round\_clip}(W_{ij}/(\beta+\epsilon), -1, +1)$. Per-tensor $\beta$, ternary output. Trained from scratch in 1.58-bit, not post-quantized.

terms.short_def: GPTQ: W4A16, layer-wise reconstruction, sequential.
terms.full_def: GPTQ: W4A16, layer-wise reconstruction, sequential. AWQ: W4A16, salient-channel preservation, no calibration backprop. SmoothQuant: W8A8, activation-outlier migration. The three occupy distinct cells of the (precision × technique) grid.

terms.short_def: IEEE-style 8-bit floating-point.
terms.full_def: IEEE-style 8-bit floating-point. E4M3: 4-exp / 3-mantissa, narrower range / better resolution (typical for activations). E5M2: 5-exp / 2-mantissa, wider range (typical for gradients). Production choice from H100 onward.

terms.short_def: Block-wise FP format: a per-block shared 8-bit exponent + per-element 4–8 bit elements (MXFP4, MXFP6, MXFP8).
terms.full_def: Block-wise FP format: a per-block shared 8-bit exponent + per-element 4–8 bit elements (MXFP4, MXFP6, MXFP8). Open standard ratified by OCP 2023.

terms.short_def: Inference acceleration by drafter–target factoring: a cheap drafter $M_q$ proposes $\gamma$ tokens; the expensive target $M_p$ scores them…
terms.full_def: Inference acceleration by drafter–target factoring: a cheap drafter $M_q$ proposes $\gamma$ tokens; the expensive target $M_p$ scores them in one parallel forward pass; a rejection-sampling rule preserves the exact target distribution.

terms.short_def: For draft token $x \sim q$: accept with probability $\min(1, p(x)/q(x))$; on rejection, sample from $\text{norm}(\max(0, p - q))$.
terms.full_def: For draft token $x \sim q$: accept with probability $\min(1, p(x)/q(x))$; on rejection, sample from $\text{norm}(\max(0, p - q))$. Guarantees the accepted token's marginal is exactly $p$.

terms.short_def: $\alpha = \mathbb{E}_{x \sim q}[\min(1, p(x)/q(x))]$.
terms.full_def: $\alpha = \mathbb{E}_{x \sim q}[\min(1, p(x)/q(x))]$. The probability a single drafter token is accepted; the dominant lever in the speedup formula. Typical values: 0.5–0.8 for well-matched drafter–target pairs.

terms.short_def: $\mathbb{E}[\text{tokens per cycle}] = (1-\alpha^{\gamma+1})/(1-\alpha)$ (Eq.1, capped geometric).
terms.full_def: $\mathbb{E}[\text{tokens per cycle}] = (1-\alpha^{\gamma+1})/(1-\alpha)$ (Eq.1, capped geometric). Walltime improvement $= [(1-\alpha^{\gamma+1})/(1-\alpha)] / (c\gamma+1)$ with $c = T_q/T_p$ (Theorem 3.8).

terms.short_def: The two LMs in speculative decoding.
terms.full_def: The two LMs in speculative decoding. Drafter $M_q$ is small/fast, target $M_p$ is large/correct. The target is the only model whose distribution matters for output quality.

terms.short_def: A draft tree of token candidates verified in one target forward pass via a custom attention mask that lets every node attend only to its an…
terms.full_def: A draft tree of token candidates verified in one target forward pass via a custom attention mask that lets every node attend only to its ancestors. Enables draft-width $> 1$ per position.

terms.short_def: $K$ extra decoding heads $h^{(k)}: \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{R}^{|V|}$ predicting tokens at offsets $t+1, t+2, \ldots, t+K$ from the same hi…
terms.full_def: $K$ extra decoding heads $h^{(k)}: \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{R}^{|V|}$ predicting tokens at offsets $t+1, t+2, \ldots, t+K$ from the same hidden state. Each head is a small residual MLP + the original LM head.

terms.short_def: Medusa-1: heads-only fine-tuning, target frozen.
terms.full_def: Medusa-1: heads-only fine-tuning, target frozen. Medusa-2: jointly fine-tunes target + heads with loss $\mathcal{L} = \mathcal{L}_{\text{LM}} + \lambda_0 \mathcal{L}_{\text{heads}}$ on a small fine-tuning set; recovers the most quality + speed.

terms.short_def: Medusa's quality–throughput knob: accept any draft token whose probability under the target exceeds $\min(\epsilon, \delta \cdot \exp(-H(p)…
terms.full_def: Medusa's quality–throughput knob: accept any draft token whose probability under the target exceeds $\min(\epsilon, \delta \cdot \exp(-H(p)))$. Trades exact-distribution preservation for higher acceptance and longer accepted runs.

terms.short_def: Drafts at the penultimate-layer hidden-state (feature) level, not the token level.
terms.full_def: Drafts at the penultimate-layer hidden-state (feature) level, not the token level. Drafter input at step $k$ is $(\text{feature}_{t+k-1}, \text{token}_{t+k})$ — the shifted-token trick disambiguates which sample produced which feature.

terms.short_def: Drafter-free speculative decoding.
terms.full_def: Drafter-free speculative decoding. Maintains a Jacobi-style n-gram pool of recently-seen completions and verifies them against the target. No drafter to train; lower acceptance than EAGLE but zero-shot deployable.

terms.short_def: Orca's contribution: schedule at the granularity of one decoder step rather than one full request, so finished requests free their slot imm…
terms.full_def: Orca's contribution: schedule at the granularity of one decoder step rather than one full request, so finished requests free their slot immediately and incoming requests join mid-batch. The throughput substrate every modern stack inherits.
