terms.short_def: The first forward pass over the full prompt; compute-bound, dominated by GEMM on the prompt-length dimension.
terms.full_def: The first forward pass over the full prompt; compute-bound, dominated by GEMM on the prompt-length dimension. Latency target: TTFT (time-to-first-token).

terms.short_def: Subsequent autoregressive token generation; memory-bound, dominated by KV-cache reads.
terms.full_def: Subsequent autoregressive token generation; memory-bound, dominated by KV-cache reads. Latency target: TPOT (time-per-output-token).

terms.short_def: Run prefill and decode on separate worker pools (Splitwise, DistServe).
terms.full_def: Run prefill and decode on separate worker pools (Splitwise, DistServe). Trades cross-machine KV-transfer overhead for the ability to size each pool to its dominant resource (compute vs memory bandwidth). Tightens TTFT and TPOT tails simultaneously.

terms.short_def: Break a long prefill into chunks of $S$ tokens, interleaving each chunk with a decode microbatch; piggybacks decode work onto the prefill c…
terms.full_def: Break a long prefill into chunks of $S$ tokens, interleaving each chunk with a decode microbatch; piggybacks decode work onto the prefill compute envelope. Improves throughput at modest TTFT cost.

terms.short_def: Time-To-First-Token and Time-Per-Output-Token.
terms.full_def: Time-To-First-Token and Time-Per-Output-Token. The two production SLOs serving systems are tuned against.

terms.short_def: IO-aware tiled-softmax attention kernel (Dao 2022/2023/2024).
terms.full_def: IO-aware tiled-softmax attention kernel (Dao 2022/2023/2024). Underneath every modern serving stack; vLLM and SGLang call into FA-2/3 for the actual attention compute, layering PagedAttention/RadixAttention on top for the KV-cache management.

terms.short_def: Mask the next-token logits at every step to allow only tokens consistent with a target grammar (regex, JSON schema, GBNF, arbitrary CFG).
terms.full_def: Mask the next-token logits at every step to allow only tokens consistent with a target grammar (regex, JSON schema, GBNF, arbitrary CFG). Naive implementations query the grammar engine for every token in the vocabulary at every step — the bottleneck modern engines fix.

terms.short_def: XGrammar's core trick: precompute (offline) the mask for tokens whose grammar-allowed-or-not status depends only on the grammar state (~99%…
terms.full_def: XGrammar's core trick: precompute (offline) the mask for tokens whose grammar-allowed-or-not status depends only on the grammar state (~99% of vocab); compute at runtime only the small remainder (~1%) that depends on the active context. Brings constrained-decoding overhead from ms/token to μs/token.

terms.short_def: When a JSON-schema or regex forces a deterministic chain of tokens (e.g. the constant prefix `{"summary": "`), merge those single-edge runs…
terms.full_def: When a JSON-schema or regex forces a deterministic chain of tokens (e.g. the constant prefix `{"summary": "`), merge those single-edge runs in the FSM so the constrained decoder emits the entire deterministic prefix in one step instead of token-by-token.

terms.short_def: Production-facing API surface (OpenAI, Gemini, vLLM, llama.cpp) where the user supplies a JSON Schema and the engine guarantees output vali…
terms.full_def: Production-facing API surface (OpenAI, Gemini, vLLM, llama.cpp) where the user supplies a JSON Schema and the engine guarantees output validates against it. Implemented via constrained decoding on the schema-derived FSM.

terms.short_def: the research program of reverse-engineering neural networks into human-readable algorithms, at the level of circuits (sub-graphs) and featu…
terms.full_def: the research program of reverse-engineering neural networks into human-readable algorithms, at the level of circuits (sub-graphs) and features (directions). Distinct from behavioral interpretability (input/output black-box evaluation) and post-hoc rationalization.

terms.short_def: a sub-graph $C$ of a model's computational graph $M$ that, when used in place of $M$ via knockouts of $M \setminus C$, approximately reprod…
terms.full_def: a sub-graph $C$ of a model's computational graph $M$ that, when used in place of $M$ via knockouts of $M \setminus C$, approximately reproduces $M$'s behavior on a target task

terms.short_def: a direction $\mathbf{d} \in \mathbb{R}^n$ in some activation space such that the projection $\mathbf{x}^\top \mathbf{d}$ (or equivalently,…
terms.full_def: a direction $\mathbf{d} \in \mathbb{R}^n$ in some activation space such that the projection $\mathbf{x}^\top \mathbf{d}$ (or equivalently, an SAE latent activation with decoder column $\mathbf{d}$) corresponds to a human-interpretable property. The unit of analysis at the *what* level; circuits operate at the *how* level.

terms.short_def: the three quantitative validity criteria for a circuit.
terms.full_def: the three quantitative validity criteria for a circuit. Faithfulness: $C$ matches $M$'s performance on the task. Completeness: $C$ contains every node needed. Minimality: every node in $C$ is necessary

terms.short_def: the property that an individual neuron responds to multiple unrelated concepts.
terms.full_def: the property that an individual neuron responds to multiple unrelated concepts. The motivation for SAEs (which lift polysemanticity by inflating the basis).

terms.short_def: the property that a feature responds to a single, human-interpretable concept.
terms.full_def: the property that a feature responds to a single, human-interpretable concept. The (contested) goal of SAE training; "Towards Monosemanticity" (Bricken 2023) is the founding reference. [CONTRADICTION] Whether SAEs achieve true monosemanticity is challenged by

terms.short_def: the hypothesis (Elhage 2022) that a network with $n$ neurons can represent more than $n$ approximately-orthogonal features as long as they…
terms.full_def: the hypothesis (Elhage 2022) that a network with $n$ neurons can represent more than $n$ approximately-orthogonal features as long as they fire sparsely. SAEs lift superposition by inflating the dictionary size $M \gg n$.

terms.short_def: the linear data bus running through a decoder-only transformer's layers; every sublayer (attention, MLP) reads from and additively writes t…
terms.full_def: the linear data bus running through a decoder-only transformer's layers; every sublayer (attention, MLP) reads from and additively writes to it. The substrate that lens techniques and SAEs decompose. Vocabulary established in Elhage et al. 2021 (Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits).

terms.short_def: the canonical causal-intervention method in MI: identify which model components matter for a behavior by swapping their activations between…
terms.full_def: the canonical causal-intervention method in MI: identify which model components matter for a behavior by swapping their activations between forward passes on paired (clean, corrupted) prompts and measuring the effect on the output `[meng2022-rome §2; kb/excerpts/meng2022-rome#sec-2; zhang2023-apatching §2.1]`. Also known as causal tracing, interchange intervention, causal mediation analysis, or representation denoising.

terms.short_def: Meng et al. 2022's specific implementation of activation patching: clean run + Gaussian-noised corrupted run + patched-with-clean-state run…
terms.full_def: Meng et al. 2022's specific implementation of activation patching: clean run + Gaussian-noised corrupted run + patched-with-clean-state run, computed over every (token, layer) cell to produce a heatmap of indirect effects

terms.short_def: the change in the output metric when the activation at a specific (component, position, layer) is restored from a cached clean run during a…
terms.full_def: the change in the output metric when the activation at a specific (component, position, layer) is restored from a cached clean run during an otherwise-corrupted forward pass. $\text{IE} = \mathbb{P}_{*,\,\text{clean }h_i^{(l)}}[r] - \mathbb{P}_*[r]$

terms.short_def: IE averaged over a dataset of paired prompts; the standard metric for component importance.
terms.full_def: IE averaged over a dataset of paired prompts; the standard metric for component importance.

terms.short_def: a refinement of activation patching that isolates the direct effect of a component $C$ on a downstream component $R$, holding fixed the ind…
terms.full_def: a refinement of activation patching that isolates the direct effect of a component $C$ on a downstream component $R$, holding fixed the indirect routes through other components. Introduced in Wang et al. 2022 (IOI)

terms.short_def: gradient-based first-order approximation of the patching effect: $\text{IE}_C \approx \langle \nabla_h m, \Delta h \rangle$.
terms.full_def: gradient-based first-order approximation of the patching effect: $\text{IE}_C \approx \langle \nabla_h m, \Delta h \rangle$. Cost: 1 forward + 1 backward pass for all components. Tier B (Nanda blog); approximation breaks in non-linear regions.

terms.short_def: the two corruption methods for activation patching.
terms.full_def: the two corruption methods for activation patching. GN adds $\mathcal{N}(0, 3\sigma_\text{textset})$ to subject-token embeddings `[meng2022-rome §2]`; STR replaces the subject with a similar token (e.g., "Eiffel Tower" → "Colosseum") preserving sequence length `[zhang2023-apatching §2.1; kb/excerpts/zhang2023-apatching#sec-2-1-corruption]`. STR is the recommended default; GN can drive activations off-distribution

terms.short_def: an activation-patching metric: $\text{LD}(r, r') = \text{Logit}(r) - \text{Logit}(r')$.
terms.full_def: an activation-patching metric: $\text{LD}(r, r') = \text{Logit}(r) - \text{Logit}(r')$. The patching effect, normalized to $[0, 1]$, is $[\text{LD}_\text{pt} - \text{LD}_*] / [\text{LD}_\text{cl} - \text{LD}_*]$ `[zhang2023-apatching §2.1 metrics; kb/excerpts/zhang2023-apatching#sec-2-1-metrics]`. Recommended over raw probability because it is sensitive to components that *push against* the correct answer.

terms.short_def: knocking out a node by replacing its activation with the mean activation across a reference distribution; preferred to zero ablation, which…
terms.full_def: knocking out a node by replacing its activation with the mean activation across a reference distribution; preferred to zero ablation, which is arbitrary

terms.short_def: generic term for setting a component's contribution to a fixed reference value (zero or mean) for circuit-validation purposes
terms.full_def: generic term for setting a component's contribution to a fixed reference value (zero or mean) for circuit-validation purposes

terms.short_def: a wide ($M \gg n$) two-layer encoder-decoder trained to reconstruct a model's activations $\mathbf{x} \in \mathbb{R}^n$ as a sparse linear…
terms.full_def: a wide ($M \gg n$) two-layer encoder-decoder trained to reconstruct a model's activations $\mathbf{x} \in \mathbb{R}^n$ as a sparse linear combination of learned dictionary directions $\{\mathbf{d}_i\}_{i=1}^M$

terms.short_def: the columns $\mathbf{d}_i$ of $\mathbf{W}_\text{dec}$; each is a feature direction in $\mathbb{R}^n$.
terms.full_def: the columns $\mathbf{d}_i$ of $\mathbf{W}_\text{dec}$; each is a feature direction in $\mathbb{R}^n$. Templeton 2024 distinguishes "feature" (the conceptual entity the model represents) from "latent" (the SAE's learned approximation).

terms.short_def: the ratio $M / n$ of dictionary size to activation dimension.
terms.full_def: the ratio $M / n$ of dictionary size to activation dimension. Typical values 8–1000.

terms.short_def: the headline sparsity metric: the number of non-zero feature activations per token.
terms.full_def: the headline sparsity metric: the number of non-zero feature activations per token. The $x$-axis of the reconstruction-fidelity Pareto plots

terms.short_def: SAE variant with $\sigma = \text{TopK}(\cdot)$: zero all but the top $K$ pre-activations.
terms.full_def: SAE variant with $\sigma = \text{TopK}(\cdot)$: zero all but the top $K$ pre-activations. Direct $L_0$ control, no L1 penalty needed. OpenAI 2024

terms.short_def: SAE variant with a discontinuous activation $\text{JumpReLU}_\theta(z) = z \cdot H(z - \theta)$ and per-feature learned threshold $\theta$.
terms.full_def: SAE variant with a discontinuous activation $\text{JumpReLU}_\theta(z) = z \cdot H(z - \theta)$ and per-feature learned threshold $\theta$. Trained with L0 penalty via straight-through estimators. SOTA reconstruction at fixed L0 on Gemma 2 9B

terms.short_def: SAE variant with two encoder kernels, one for gating (binary on/off) and one for magnitude estimation.
terms.full_def: SAE variant with two encoder kernels, one for gating (binary on/off) and one for magnitude estimation. Architecturally equivalent (with weight sharing) to JumpReLU

terms.short_def: a backward-pass surrogate used to backprop through the discontinuous JumpReLU and the L0 penalty.
terms.full_def: a backward-pass surrogate used to backprop through the discontinuous JumpReLU and the L0 penalty. The forward pass uses the discontinuity; the backward pass uses a smooth (kernel-density-estimated) gradient

terms.short_def: an SAE feature that has stopped activating across the training distribution (typically because its encoder weights collapsed during early t…
terms.full_def: an SAE feature that has stopped activating across the training distribution (typically because its encoder weights collapsed during early training). Up to 90% of latents in a large SAE can be dead without mitigation

terms.short_def: auxiliary loss added by Gao et al. 2024 to revive dead latents: model the residual reconstruction error using the top-$k_\text{aux}$ dead l…
terms.full_def: auxiliary loss added by Gao et al. 2024 to revive dead latents: model the residual reconstruction error using the top-$k_\text{aux}$ dead latents

terms.short_def: the bias introduced by the L1 penalty in ReLU SAEs: positive feature activations are systematically underestimated, so reconstructed activa…
terms.full_def: the bias introduced by the L1 penalty in ReLU SAEs: positive feature activations are systematically underestimated, so reconstructed activations have smaller magnitudes than true values. Avoided by TopK and JumpReLU

terms.short_def: Leask et al. 2025: insert/swap latents from a larger SAE into a smaller one to compare bases.
terms.full_def: Leask et al. 2025: insert/swap latents from a larger SAE into a smaller one to compare bases. Reveals novel latents (in the larger but not the smaller SAE) and reconstruction latents (in both with similar behavior)

terms.short_def: an SAE trained on the *decoder matrix* of another SAE.
terms.full_def: an SAE trained on the *decoder matrix* of another SAE. Reveals that latents in a larger SAE often decompose into sparse combinations of meta-latents, contradicting the "atomic features" assumption

terms.short_def: the (challenged) hypothesis that SAEs find a unique, complete, atomic decomposition of model activations into features.
terms.full_def: the (challenged) hypothesis that SAEs find a unique, complete, atomic decomposition of model activations into features. [CONTRADICTION] Leask et al. 2025 argue against canonicality with both stitching and meta-SAE evidence

terms.short_def: a wide ReLU MLP (encoder–decoder) trained to approximate the input/output behavior of an MLP sublayer: $\text{TC}(\mathbf{x}) = \mathbf{W}_…
terms.full_def: a wide ReLU MLP (encoder–decoder) trained to approximate the input/output behavior of an MLP sublayer: $\text{TC}(\mathbf{x}) = \mathbf{W}_\text{dec}\,\text{ReLU}(\mathbf{W}_\text{enc} \mathbf{x} + \mathbf{b}_\text{enc}) + \mathbf{b}_\text{dec}$, with L1 sparsity penalty on the hidden activations `[dunefsky2024-transcoders §3.1 Eq.3-5; kb/excerpts/dunefsky2024-transcoders#sec-3-1]`. Distinct from an SAE: a transcoder approximates a *function* (the MLP); an SAE approximates a *vector* (an activation).

terms.short_def: Lindsey et al. 2025: a transcoder whose decoder writes into the residual stream of *all subsequent* layers, not just the immediate next one.
terms.full_def: Lindsey et al. 2025: a transcoder whose decoder writes into the residual stream of *all subsequent* layers, not just the immediate next one. Makes a feature's skip-layer contributions explicit in the attribution graph. HTML-only at transformer-circuits.pub.

terms.short_def: the property of a transcoder (and *not* of an SAE-on-MLP-output) that the connections between features can be stated independently of any s…
terms.full_def: the property of a transcoder (and *not* of an SAE-on-MLP-output) that the connections between features can be stated independently of any specific input. Necessary for weight-based circuit analysis through MLPs

terms.short_def: the output of circuit tracing: a sparse directed graph from input embeddings to output unembedding, with feature activations as nodes and J…
terms.full_def: the output of circuit tracing: a sparse directed graph from input embeddings to output unembedding, with feature activations as nodes and Jacobian-weighted attribution scores as edges. Computed in 1 forward + 1 backward pass on the transcoder-replaced model (Lindsey 2025).

terms.short_def: Anthropic's 2025 methodology: replace MLPs with cross-layer transcoders, then compute attribution graphs by Jacobian back-attribution from…
terms.full_def: Anthropic's 2025 methodology: replace MLPs with cross-layer transcoders, then compute attribution graphs by Jacobian back-attribution from a target node. Synthesis of SAE feature decomposition + activation patching causal intervention.

terms.short_def: apply the final LayerNorm + unembedding $W_U$ to an intermediate residual-stream activation $\mathbf{h}^{(\ell)}$ to read out a layer-$\ell…
terms.full_def: apply the final LayerNorm + unembedding $W_U$ to an intermediate residual-stream activation $\mathbf{h}^{(\ell)}$ to read out a layer-$\ell$ vocabulary distribution. nostalgebraist 2020 (LessWrong, Tier B). Formal expression and decomposition in `[belrose2023-tuned-lens §2 Eq.3]` (PDF p.2).

terms.short_def: Belrose et al. 2023: per-layer affine "translators" $\mathbf{h}_\ell \mapsto A_\ell \mathbf{h}_\ell + b_\ell$ trained with a distillation l…
terms.full_def: Belrose et al. 2023: per-layer affine "translators" $\mathbf{h}_\ell \mapsto A_\ell \mathbf{h}_\ell + b_\ell$ trained with a distillation loss against final-layer logits. More reliable than the raw logit lens, especially on BLOOM/GPT-Neo

terms.short_def: Belrose et al. 2023's algorithm for finding residual-stream directions with highest influence on the tuned lens output.
terms.full_def: Belrose et al. 2023's algorithm for finding residual-stream directions with highest influence on the tuned lens output. Connection between lens analysis and MI feature attribution.

terms.short_def: *Decoding by Contrasting Layers*: an inference-time application of lens analysis.
terms.full_def: *Decoding by Contrasting Layers*: an inference-time application of lens analysis. The decoded logits are $\text{logit}^{(L)}(t) - \text{logit}^{(\ell^*)}(t)$ for an adaptively chosen early layer $\ell^*$, improving factuality. Chuang et al. 2023.

terms.short_def: a (typically linear) classifier $g(\mathbf{h}) = \sigma(\mathbf{w}^\top \mathbf{h} + b)$ trained on labeled activations to predict an exter…
terms.full_def: a (typically linear) classifier $g(\mathbf{h}) = \sigma(\mathbf{w}^\top \mathbf{h} + b)$ trained on labeled activations to predict an external property $y$. Foundational reference: Alain & Bengio 2017.

terms.short_def: Marks & Tegmark 2023: define the probe direction as $\mathbf{d}_\text{class} = \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{h} \mid y = c_1] - \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{h}…
terms.full_def: Marks & Tegmark 2023: define the probe direction as $\mathbf{d}_\text{class} = \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{h} \mid y = c_1] - \mathbb{E}[\mathbf{h} \mid y = c_0]$, then project activations onto $\mathbf{d}_\text{class}$. Simpler and more generalization-robust than logistic regression on the same features

terms.short_def: the canonical mass-mean probe direction for truth-value of a statement, derived in Marks & Tegmark 2023.
terms.full_def: the canonical mass-mean probe direction for truth-value of a statement, derived in Marks & Tegmark 2023. A small linear subspace separates true and false statements across many models and datasets.

terms.short_def: a probe demonstrates that the model *represents* a property; an activation patch along the probe direction demonstrates that the model *use…
terms.full_def: a probe demonstrates that the model *represents* a property; an activation patch along the probe direction demonstrates that the model *uses* it. The 2023+ literature is converging on always pairing the two.

terms.short_def: The 3-stage post-training pipeline: SFT → reward-model training on pairwise preferences → PPO with KL anchor against $\pi^{\mathrm{SFT}}$.
terms.full_def: The 3-stage post-training pipeline: SFT → reward-model training on pairwise preferences → PPO with KL anchor against $\pi^{\mathrm{SFT}}$. Canonical reference is InstructGPT

terms.short_def: Assumes pairwise preferences depend on a difference of latent rewards: $p(y_w \succ y_l|x) = \sigma(r(x,y_w) - r(x,y_l))$.
terms.full_def: Assumes pairwise preferences depend on a difference of latent rewards: $p(y_w \succ y_l|x) = \sigma(r(x,y_w) - r(x,y_l))$. The basis for both the RM training loss and DPO

terms.short_def: A neural network trained on Bradley–Terry preferences to score response quality.
terms.full_def: A neural network trained on Bradley–Terry preferences to score response quality. Initialized from $\pi^{\mathrm{SFT}}$ with a scalar head replacing the LM head. Used as the reward signal for PPO

terms.short_def: InstructGPT's RLHF objective with an added pre-training-mixture term: $+\gamma\, \mathbb{E}_{\mathrm{pretrain}}[\log\pi_\theta]$.
terms.full_def: InstructGPT's RLHF objective with an added pre-training-mixture term: $+\gamma\, \mathbb{E}_{\mathrm{pretrain}}[\log\pi_\theta]$. Reduces alignment tax on standard NLP benchmarks

terms.short_def: The $\beta \log\pi_\theta(y|x)/\pi^{\mathrm{SFT}}(y|x)$ term in RLHF objectives that prevents the policy from drifting too far from the SFT…
terms.full_def: The $\beta \log\pi_\theta(y|x)/\pi^{\mathrm{SFT}}(y|x)$ term in RLHF objectives that prevents the policy from drifting too far from the SFT initialization. Implemented per-token in the reward in InstructGPT-style PPO

terms.short_def: The performance regression on standard NLP benchmarks observed after RLHF.
terms.full_def: The performance regression on standard NLP benchmarks observed after RLHF. PPO-ptx mitigates it by mixing in pre-training likelihood

terms.short_def: Average parameters of $K$ independently-trained RMs to reduce overoptimization and improve robustness.
terms.full_def: Average parameters of $K$ independently-trained RMs to reduce overoptimization and improve robustness. Adopted in Gemma 3 post-training

terms.short_def: Alternates RL training with policy weight averaging; companion to WARM at the policy side.
terms.full_def: Alternates RL training with policy weight averaging; companion to WARM at the policy side. Used together in Gemma 3

terms.short_def: The gap between proxy reward $r_\phi$ and gold reward grows as KL($\pi_\theta\|\pi_{\mathrm{ref}}$) grows; the policy "hacks" the RM, scori…
terms.full_def: The gap between proxy reward $r_\phi$ and gold reward grows as KL($\pi_\theta\|\pi_{\mathrm{ref}}$) grows; the policy "hacks" the RM, scoring high on $r_\phi$ while degrading on true quality. Affects both RLHF (Gao et al. 2023) and DPO-class methods

terms.short_def: Policy exploits an artifact of the reward signal that does not correspond to true quality (e.g., verbosity, sycophancy, format-shortcuts).
terms.full_def: Policy exploits an artifact of the reward signal that does not correspond to true quality (e.g., verbosity, sycophancy, format-shortcuts). Distinct from overoptimization in that hacking can occur even at low KL

terms.short_def: Closed-form, classification-loss reformulation of RLHF: trains the policy directly on preference pairs by inverting the closed-form RL opti…
terms.full_def: Closed-form, classification-loss reformulation of RLHF: trains the policy directly on preference pairs by inverting the closed-form RL optimum. No reward model, no on-policy sampling, no PPO loop

terms.short_def: In DPO, the policy log-ratio $\hat{r}_\theta(x,y) = \beta\log\pi_\theta(y|x)/\pi_{\mathrm{ref}}(y|x)$ that plays the role of a reward funct…
terms.full_def: In DPO, the policy log-ratio $\hat{r}_\theta(x,y) = \beta\log\pi_\theta(y|x)/\pi_{\mathrm{ref}}(y|x)$ that plays the role of a reward function. The policy is trained on preferences over implicit-reward differences

terms.short_def: The closed-form mapping between optimal policy and reward (Eq. 4 of Rafailov 2023): $\pi^*(y|x) \propto \pi_{\mathrm{ref}}(y|x)\exp(r(x,y)/…
terms.full_def: The closed-form mapping between optimal policy and reward (Eq. 4 of Rafailov 2023): $\pi^*(y|x) \propto \pi_{\mathrm{ref}}(y|x)\exp(r(x,y)/\beta)$. Inverting this gives the implicit reward

terms.short_def: DPO-trained models tend to produce longer outputs than the reference because long responses are easier to satisfy with high log-ratios.
terms.full_def: DPO-trained models tend to produce longer outputs than the reference because long responses are easier to satisfy with high log-ratios. Addressed by SimPO's length normalization

terms.short_def: DPO variant replacing log-sigmoid loss with squared loss on the preference gap, addressing DPO's overconfidence pathology
terms.full_def: DPO variant replacing log-sigmoid loss with squared loss on the preference gap, addressing DPO's overconfidence pathology

terms.short_def: Operates on single-rating data $(x,y,\mathrm{label}\in\{\text{good},\text{bad}\})$ instead of pairwise preferences.
terms.full_def: Operates on single-rating data $(x,y,\mathrm{label}\in\{\text{good},\text{bad}\})$ instead of pairwise preferences. Asymmetric prospect-theoretic loss with loss aversion

terms.short_def: Combines SFT and preference losses in a single objective; no reference model, no SFT stage.
terms.full_def: Combines SFT and preference losses in a single objective; no reference model, no SFT stage. Trains from base directly via log-odds ratio of SFT loss between chosen and rejected responses

terms.short_def: Reference-free DPO variant using length-normalized average log-prob as implicit reward, plus a target reward margin $\gamma$.
terms.full_def: Reference-free DPO variant using length-normalized average log-prob as implicit reward, plus a target reward margin $\gamma$. Eliminates DPO length bias on length-controlled benchmarks

terms.short_def: Axis along which DPO variants split.
terms.full_def: Axis along which DPO variants split. Reference-anchored: DPO, IPO, KTO. Reference-free: ORPO, SimPO, CPO. Reference-anchored variants depend on $\pi_{\mathrm{ref}}(y|x)$ in the loss; reference-free variants use only $\pi_\theta$

terms.short_def: Multi-round pipeline: run DPO, sample new responses, label them, repeat.
terms.full_def: Multi-round pipeline: run DPO, sample new responses, label them, repeat. Llama-3 and Tülu 3 reference recipe

terms.short_def: Replace human preference labels in RLHF with AI-generated preference labels, typically from a feedback model conditioned on a written const…
terms.full_def: Replace human preference labels in RLHF with AI-generated preference labels, typically from a feedback model conditioned on a written constitution

terms.short_def: Anthropic 2022 framework for training harmless assistants without human harm labels.
terms.full_def: Anthropic 2022 framework for training harmless assistants without human harm labels. Two stages: SL-CAI (sample → critique under principle → revise → SFT) and RL-CAI (sample pairs → AI judge under principle → train RM → PPO)

terms.short_def: A list of principles ("the assistant should not be racist," etc.) used by the feedback model to evaluate / critique responses in CAI.
terms.full_def: A list of principles ("the assistant should not be racist," etc.) used by the feedback model to evaluate / critique responses in CAI. Typical size: ~16 principles

terms.short_def: Supervised learning stage of Constitutional AI.
terms.full_def: Supervised learning stage of Constitutional AI. Sample initial responses, critique them under a randomly-chosen principle, revise, and SFT on revised responses

terms.short_def: Reinforcement learning stage of Constitutional AI.
terms.full_def: Reinforcement learning stage of Constitutional AI. Generate AI preferences by having a feedback model choose between response pairs under a principle, train a reward model on AI preferences, run PPO

terms.short_def: Inverts the constitution for adversarial / toxic data generation; probability-clamped RLAIF for red-team data synthesis (April 2026)
terms.full_def: Inverts the constitution for adversarial / toxic data generation; probability-clamped RLAIF for red-team data synthesis (April 2026)

terms.short_def: Post-training paradigm where the reward is a deterministic programmatic verifier (math equality, code unit-tests, format check), not a lear…
terms.full_def: Post-training paradigm where the reward is a deterministic programmatic verifier (math equality, code unit-tests, format check), not a learned reward model. The policy still uses on-policy RL

terms.short_def: A 0/1 reward computed by a programmatic check: $R(x,y) = \mathbb{1}[\mathrm{verify}(x,y)]$.
terms.full_def: A 0/1 reward computed by a programmatic check: $R(x,y) = \mathbb{1}[\mathrm{verify}(x,y)]$. Sparse and terminal

terms.short_def: Critic-free PPO variant: replaces the value model with a group-mean reward baseline.
terms.full_def: Critic-free PPO variant: replaces the value model with a group-mean reward baseline. Sample $G$ trajectories per prompt, normalize rewards within the group, use the standardized advantage in the PPO loss. Used by DeepSeek-R1, Qwen 3, Tülu 3 RLVR

terms.short_def: In GRPO, $\tilde{A}_i = (R_i - \mathrm{mean}(\{R_j\}))/\mathrm{std}(\{R_j\})$, broadcast to every token of trajectory $o_i$.
terms.full_def: In GRPO, $\tilde{A}_i = (R_i - \mathrm{mean}(\{R_j\}))/\mathrm{std}(\{R_j\})$, broadcast to every token of trajectory $o_i$. Replaces GAE-derived per-token advantages

terms.short_def: In RLVR, a small constant reward for trajectories that conform to a structural template (e.g., emit a `<think>...</think>` block).
terms.full_def: In RLVR, a small constant reward for trajectories that conform to a structural template (e.g., emit a `<think>...</think>` block). Keeps the chain-of-thought pathway alive during early training

aliases: Decoupled clip and dynamic sAmpling Policy Optimization
terms.short_def: GRPO improvement with four targeted modifications: Clip-Higher (asymmetric PPO clip), Dynamic Sampling (reject zero-gradient groups), Token…
terms.full_def: GRPO improvement with four targeted modifications: Clip-Higher (asymmetric PPO clip), Dynamic Sampling (reject zero-gradient groups), Token-Level Loss (equal-weight tokens regardless of trajectory length), Overlong Reward Shaping (soft penalty for length-truncated trajectories)

terms.short_def: DAPO's asymmetric PPO clip $[1-\epsilon_{\text{low}}, 1+\epsilon_{\text{high}}]$ with $\epsilon_{\text{high}} > \epsilon_{\text{low}}$.
terms.full_def: DAPO's asymmetric PPO clip $[1-\epsilon_{\text{low}}, 1+\epsilon_{\text{high}}]$ with $\epsilon_{\text{high}} > \epsilon_{\text{low}}$. Allows larger upward updates on under-probable correct tokens

terms.short_def: Reject groups where all $G$ trajectories share the same reward, since the GRPO advantage is then zero and the gradient is wasted.
terms.full_def: Reject groups where all $G$ trajectories share the same reward, since the GRPO advantage is then zero and the gradient is wasted. Re-sample until each group contains both $R=0$ and $R=1$ trajectories

terms.short_def: RL variant that re-introduces a learned value function on top of GRPO's design, with shared backbone and value-loss-clipping.
terms.full_def: RL variant that re-introduces a learned value function on top of GRPO's design, with shared backbone and value-loss-clipping. Reportedly outperforms DAPO on long-CoT tasks

terms.short_def: PPO variant using per-prefix Monte Carlo re-simulation as value estimate.
terms.full_def: PPO variant using per-prefix Monte Carlo re-simulation as value estimate. Drops the value network. 3× wall-clock speedup over PPO on MATH/GSM8K

terms.short_def: A family of REINFORCE-class methods that drop PPO's clip but keep the GRPO group-mean baseline and KL anchor.
terms.full_def: A family of REINFORCE-class methods that drop PPO's clip but keep the GRPO group-mean baseline and KL anchor. Whether clip helps in this regime is contested.

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-R1-Zero, the version of R1 trained with **no SFT cold-start** — pure GRPO RL applied directly to the DeepSeek-V3 base.
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-R1-Zero, the version of R1 trained with **no SFT cold-start** — pure GRPO RL applied directly to the DeepSeek-V3 base. Achieves AIME 2024 pass@1 of 77.9%

terms.short_def: A spike in the model's use of reflection words like "wait" during training, taken as evidence of self-reorganization of reasoning structure
terms.full_def: A spike in the model's use of reflection words like "wait" during training, taken as evidence of self-reorganization of reasoning structure

terms.short_def: Pass@K is the probability that at least one of $K$ independent samples from the model passes the verifier.
terms.full_def: Pass@K is the probability that at least one of $K$ independent samples from the model passes the verifier. Pass@1 is the single-sample success rate. RLVR primarily improves pass@1 (sampling efficiency)

terms.short_def: Empirical finding (NeurIPS 2025 oral, Yue et al.) that RLVR improves pass@1 on problems the base model can already solve at higher K, but d…
terms.full_def: Empirical finding (NeurIPS 2025 oral, Yue et al.) that RLVR improves pass@1 on problems the base model can already solve at higher K, but does not expand the set of problems solvable at any K. The capability ceiling is set by pre-training

terms.short_def: Llama-3 SFT quality gate: sample $N$ candidates from the model, score by reward model, keep top-$k$, SFT on those.
terms.full_def: Llama-3 SFT quality gate: sample $N$ candidates from the model, score by reward model, keep top-$k$, SFT on those. Used as a training-loop stage in modern multi-round pipelines

terms.short_def: Alignment data synthesis from scratch: prompt aligned LLMs with only their instruction-template prefix (no question), have them fabricate t…
terms.full_def: Alignment data synthesis from scratch: prompt aligned LLMs with only their instruction-template prefix (no question), have them fabricate the question first, then sample the answer. Near-zero-cost SFT data generation

terms.short_def: A small SFT stage on long-CoT examples preceding the main RL stage.
terms.full_def: A small SFT stage on long-CoT examples preceding the main RL stage. Provides a reasoning-format and language-quality regularizer for the subsequent RL. Distinct from full SFT pipelines

terms.short_def: Training small models (1.5B–32B) on reasoning trajectories generated by a strong reasoning teacher (R1, o1) via pure SFT with no RL.
terms.full_def: Training small models (1.5B–32B) on reasoning trajectories generated by a strong reasoning teacher (R1, o1) via pure SFT with no RL. The 800K-sample DeepSeek-R1 distillation set is the canonical example

terms.short_def: Two distinct response styles trained with separate SFT data and selectable at inference via system prompt: "thinking" emits a `<think>...</…
terms.full_def: Two distinct response styles trained with separate SFT data and selectable at inference via system prompt: "thinking" emits a `<think>...</think>` block, "non-thinking" produces direct answers

terms.short_def: The canonical 4-stage open post-training reference: data curation → SFT → DPO → RLVR.
terms.full_def: The canonical 4-stage open post-training reference: data curation → SFT → DPO → RLVR. Tülu 3 405B (Jan 2025) scales RLVR to 405B parameters

terms.short_def: Multi-round pipeline used by Llama-3 and Tülu 3: SFT, then sample candidates, run rejection sampling, run DPO, repeat with the new policy a…
terms.full_def: Multi-round pipeline used by Llama-3 and Tülu 3: SFT, then sample candidates, run rejection sampling, run DPO, repeat with the new policy as starting point

terms.short_def: A prompting and decoding pattern in which the model produces an intermediate sequence of reasoning steps $z$ before emitting a final answer…
terms.full_def: A prompting and decoding pattern in which the model produces an intermediate sequence of reasoning steps $z$ before emitting a final answer $y$. In few-shot CoT (Wei 2022), the prompt contains exemplars with hand-written reasoning traces; in zero-shot CoT (Kojima 2022), the trigger phrase "Let's think step by step" elicits the same behaviour without exemplars.

terms.short_def: The Wei 2022 exemplar-based variant.
terms.full_def: The Wei 2022 exemplar-based variant. The prompt contains $K$ demonstrations $(x_i, z_i, y_i)$ before the query. CoT gain over direct prompting is **emergent at scale** — sub-100B models often do not benefit.

terms.short_def: The Kojima 2022 trigger-phrase variant.
terms.full_def: The Kojima 2022 trigger-phrase variant. Two-stage decode: reasoning extraction + answer extraction. Recovers a substantial fraction of few-shot CoT gain at instruction-tuned scale without exemplars.

terms.short_def: Sample $N$ reasoning traces at temperature $T > 0$, majority-vote over extracted answers.
terms.full_def: Sample $N$ reasoning traces at temperature $T > 0$, majority-vote over extracted answers. The simplest test-time-compute strategy. Gains: GSM8K +17.9%, SVAMP +11.0% over greedy CoT.

terms.short_def: The behaviour of a reasoning-trained model (o1, R1, QwQ) that emits long reasoning traces unconditionally, without prompt scaffolding.
terms.full_def: The behaviour of a reasoning-trained model (o1, R1, QwQ) that emits long reasoning traces unconditionally, without prompt scaffolding. Distinct from prompted CoT in that the trace is the model's natural output distribution.

terms.short_def: The extent to which the trace $z$ causally drives the answer $y$ versus being post-hoc rationalisation.
terms.full_def: The extent to which the trace $z$ causally drives the answer $y$ versus being post-hoc rationalisation. Measured by counterfactual perturbation. Thinking models report rates ~0.04–0.14% post-hoc; non-thinking models ~7–13%.

terms.short_def: The inference-side compute spent on a single problem instance to improve answer quality, beyond a single greedy forward pass.
terms.full_def: The inference-side compute spent on a single problem instance to improve answer quality, beyond a single greedy forward pass. Recognised in 2024 as a co-equal scaling axis with training compute.

terms.short_def: The strategy maximising expected verifier score at fixed inference compute $C$.
terms.full_def: The strategy maximising expected verifier score at fixed inference compute $C$. Snell et al. 2024 show the optimal mix is problem-difficulty-dependent: easy → short single trace; medium → sampling-based; hard → search.

terms.short_def: Draw $N$ independent traces, aggregate.
terms.full_def: Draw $N$ independent traces, aggregate. Three rules: self-consistency (majority vote), best-of-$N$ + ORM, best-of-$N$ + PRM. Linear in $N$.

terms.short_def: Tree expansion over partial reasoning prefixes, directed by a learned value function.
terms.full_def: Tree expansion over partial reasoning prefixes, directed by a learned value function. Beam, BFS, MCTS variants. See `kb/notes/reasoning/inference-time-search.md`.

terms.short_def: Hold $N = 1$ but emit a much longer single trace.
terms.full_def: Hold $N = 1$ but emit a much longer single trace. The o1/R1/QwQ paradigm. Compute scales super-linearly in trace length due to KV-cache re-attention.

terms.short_def: The s1 2025 mechanism.
terms.full_def: The s1 2025 mechanism. When the model emits its end-of-thinking token before the budget is reached, suppress it and append the continuation token "Wait" to force more reasoning; hard-truncate to enforce shorter budgets.

terms.short_def: Productised TTC control: API-level integer (Gemini 2.5 `thinkingBudget`), effort level (OpenAI o-series), or token cap (Anthropic thinking…
terms.full_def: Productised TTC control: API-level integer (Gemini 2.5 `thinkingBudget`), effort level (OpenAI o-series), or token cap (Anthropic thinking block).

terms.short_def: A scoring function $R^O_\phi: (x, z, y) \to [0, 1]$ trained on (prompt, complete solution, outcome correctness) triples.
terms.full_def: A scoring function $R^O_\phi: (x, z, y) \to [0, 1]$ trained on (prompt, complete solution, outcome correctness) triples. Sparse signal — one label per trace.

terms.short_def: A scoring function $R^P_\phi: (x, s_{1:t}) \to [0, 1]$ trained on per-step correctness labels.
terms.full_def: A scoring function $R^P_\phi: (x, s_{1:t}) \to [0, 1]$ trained on per-step correctness labels. Dense signal — one label per step. Outperforms ORM at matched best-of-$N$ on MATH.

terms.short_def: OpenAI's released dataset of $\approx 800{,}000$ human step-correctness labels on GPT-4-generated MATH solutions.
terms.full_def: OpenAI's released dataset of $\approx 800{,}000$ human step-correctness labels on GPT-4-generated MATH solutions. The foundational reference dataset for PRM training.

terms.short_def: How per-step PRM scores are combined to a trace score.
terms.full_def: How per-step PRM scores are combined to a trace score. Min, product, last-step. **Min** is the strongest aggregator at PRM800K-grade.

terms.short_def: A classifier-style PRM: outputs a probability of step correctness in one feed-forward pass.
terms.full_def: A classifier-style PRM: outputs a probability of step correctness in one feed-forward pass. Cheap to evaluate; classical Math-Shepherd / OpenAI PRM lineage.

terms.short_def: A small reasoning-LLM verifier that emits its own short CoT evaluating each step before producing a verdict.
terms.full_def: A small reasoning-LLM verifier that emits its own short CoT evaluating each step before producing a verdict. Trained on 1% of PRM800K, beats full-PRM800K discriminative PRMs on ProcessBench / AIME 2024.

terms.short_def: rStar-Math's variant: trained on preferences over MCTS-discovered step pairs, rather than absolute step labels.
terms.full_def: rStar-Math's variant: trained on preferences over MCTS-discovered step pairs, rather than absolute step labels. Avoids naïve step-score annotation.

terms.short_def: Standard benchmark for PRM step-error detection (identify the first incorrect step in a partial trace).
terms.full_def: Standard benchmark for PRM step-error detection (identify the first incorrect step in a partial trace).

terms.short_def: Sample $N$ traces, score each with a verifier (ORM, PRM, or self-consistency baseline), select the highest-scored.
terms.full_def: Sample $N$ traces, score each with a verifier (ORM, PRM, or self-consistency baseline), select the highest-scored. Canonical sampling-based TTC.

terms.short_def: RL post-training where the reward is computed mechanically from the emitted answer (regex match for math, unit-test execution for code, pro…
terms.full_def: RL post-training where the reward is computed mechanically from the emitted answer (regex match for math, unit-test execution for code, proof-checker for formal proofs), not from a learned reward model. The defining feature of post-2024 reasoning training.

terms.short_def: Critic-free PPO variant (Shao 2024).
terms.full_def: Critic-free PPO variant (Shao 2024). Per-prompt group of $G$ samples; advantage is each sample's reward minus group mean, divided by group std. Eliminates the value-network requirement.

terms.short_def: ByteDance's GRPO variant.
terms.full_def: ByteDance's GRPO variant. Four modifications: clip-higher (asymmetric clip), dynamic sampling (drop zero-variance groups), token-level loss (vs sequence-level), overlong reward shaping. AIME 2024 50pts on Qwen2.5-32B.

terms.short_def: A small-scale ($\sim 10^4$ examples) SFT pass on long-CoT exemplars, run before RL to fix readability problems of pure-RL outputs (R1-Zero).
terms.full_def: A small-scale ($\sim 10^4$ examples) SFT pass on long-CoT exemplars, run before RL to fix readability problems of pure-RL outputs (R1-Zero). Does not change asymptotic capability but improves trace coherence.

terms.short_def: Generate candidate solutions with the current policy, filter by verifier (and optional quality filter), SFT on the survivors.
terms.full_def: Generate candidate solutions with the current policy, filter by verifier (and optional quality filter), SFT on the survivors. Used as Stage 3 of the R1 pipeline.

terms.short_def: Use a strong teacher's RL-trained traces as SFT data for smaller students.
terms.full_def: Use a strong teacher's RL-trained traces as SFT data for smaller students. R1 demonstrates this beats running RL directly on a small model.

terms.short_def: The empirical finding that RLVR improves $\mathrm{pass}@1$ on problems the base model could already solve at $\mathrm{pass}@k$ for some $k$…
terms.full_def: The empirical finding that RLVR improves $\mathrm{pass}@1$ on problems the base model could already solve at $\mathrm{pass}@k$ for some $k$, but does not expand the set of solvable problems. Set at pre-training.

terms.short_def: Failure mode in which the policy learns to exploit imperfections in the verifier (or PRM) rather than improving on the underlying task.
terms.full_def: Failure mode in which the policy learns to exploit imperfections in the verifier (or PRM) rather than improving on the underlying task.

terms.short_def: A single model that toggles between long-CoT and short-answer modes via system-prompt instruction.
terms.full_def: A single model that toggles between long-CoT and short-answer modes via system-prompt instruction. Productisation step of reasoning training.

terms.short_def: TTC family that constructs and explores a tree (or DAG) of partial reasoning prefixes, using a verifier or value function to direct expansi…
terms.full_def: TTC family that constructs and explores a tree (or DAG) of partial reasoning prefixes, using a verifier or value function to direct expansion. Beam / BFS / MCTS variants.

terms.short_def: Monte Carlo Tree Search where actions are next-step reasoning generations and the value function is a PRM.
terms.full_def: Monte Carlo Tree Search where actions are next-step reasoning generations and the value function is a PRM. Selection-expansion-simulation-backup loop, UCT-style exploration.

terms.short_def: rStar-Math's two-SLM design: policy SLM generates candidate next-steps, reward / discriminator SLM scores them.
terms.full_def: rStar-Math's two-SLM design: policy SLM generates candidate next-steps, reward / discriminator SLM scores them. Both small (~7B); together rival o1-mini on MATH.

terms.short_def: Self-training loop using MCTS to generate correct trajectories, then SFT on those, then update the value function.
terms.full_def: Self-training loop using MCTS to generate correct trajectories, then SFT on those, then update the value function. Structurally analogous to AlphaZero self-play.

terms.short_def: Beam search variant: from each beam candidate, perform short rollouts and score the rolled-out outcomes, then prune.
terms.full_def: Beam search variant: from each beam candidate, perform short rollouts and score the rolled-out outcomes, then prune. The form Snell 2024 uses for compute-optimal mix experiments.

terms.short_def: MCTS extended over retrieval decisions in addition to reasoning steps.
terms.full_def: MCTS extended over retrieval decisions in addition to reasoning steps. Bridges search-based TTC and retrieval-augmented generation.

terms.short_def: An empirical or theoretical functional relationship between a measure of model performance (typically cross-entropy loss) and one or more "…
terms.full_def: An empirical or theoretical functional relationship between a measure of model performance (typically cross-entropy loss) and one or more "scale" inputs: parameters $N$, training tokens $D$, training compute $C$, or test-time compute $N_{\text{test}}$. Canonical reference: `kaplan2020`.

terms.short_def: Total floating-point operations spent in pre-training, denoted $C$ or $C_{\text{train}}$.
terms.full_def: Total floating-point operations spent in pre-training, denoted $C$ or $C_{\text{train}}$. The standard accounting per-token is $C \approx 6 N D$ for dense Transformers

terms.short_def: Petaflop-day, $10^{15} \times 86{,}400 \approx 8.64 \times 10^{19}$ floating-point operations.
terms.full_def: Petaflop-day, $10^{15} \times 86{,}400 \approx 8.64 \times 10^{19}$ floating-point operations. Unit used in Kaplan 2020 for compute reporting.

terms.short_def: A relationship of the form $L(X) \propto (X_c / X)^{\alpha_X}$ with exponent $\alpha_X$.
terms.full_def: A relationship of the form $L(X) \propto (X_c / X)^{\alpha_X}$ with exponent $\alpha_X$. Holds approximately for LLM loss against $N$, $D$, and $C$ across many orders of magnitude

terms.short_def: The largest training batch (in tokens) at which scaling further is still time-efficient.
terms.full_def: The largest training batch (in tokens) at which scaling further is still time-efficient. Itself a power law in the loss: $B_{\text{crit}}(L) = B_\ast / L^{1/\alpha_B}$ with $B_\ast \approx 2 \times 10^8$ tokens, $\alpha_B \approx 0.21$

terms.short_def: The plot of training loss vs. parameter count $N$ at a fixed training-compute budget $C$.
terms.full_def: The plot of training loss vs. parameter count $N$ at a fixed training-compute budget $C$. The minimum across $N$ at each $C$ defines the compute-optimal frontier

terms.short_def: Training a model with $(N, D)$ chosen to minimize loss at fixed $C \approx 6ND$.
terms.full_def: Training a model with $(N, D)$ chosen to minimize loss at fixed $C \approx 6ND$. Chinchilla's headline rule: at the optimum, $D \approx 20 N$ for a wide range of $N$

$D / N$. Chinchilla-optimal value is approximately 20. Industry practice has shifted to over-trained ratios (Llama 3 8B ≈ 1875) trading pre-training compute for inference cost. See `scaling/scaling-frontier`.

terms.short_def: The functional form $\hat{L}(N, D) = E + A/N^\alpha + B/D^\beta$ with five fitted constants.
terms.full_def: The functional form $\hat{L}(N, D) = E + A/N^\alpha + B/D^\beta$ with five fitted constants. $E$ ≈ entropy of natural text on the eval distribution

terms.short_def: The (now superseded) prescription $N \propto C^{0.73}$, $D \propto C^{0.27}$ from `[kaplan2020 §1.2; kb/excerpts/kaplan2020#sec-1-2-compute…
terms.full_def: The (now superseded) prescription $N \propto C^{0.73}$, $D \propto C^{0.27}$ from `[kaplan2020 §1.2; kb/excerpts/kaplan2020#sec-1-2-compute-allocation]`. Overturned by Chinchilla's roughly equal-scaling result. Kaplan's flaw was an LR-schedule horizon held fixed across model sizes.

terms.short_def: Community term for the practical problem that a Chinchilla-compute-optimal model is often too large to *serve* economically.
terms.full_def: Community term for the practical problem that a Chinchilla-compute-optimal model is often too large to *serve* economically. Models destined for high-volume deployment over-train (small $N$, large $D$) to minimize inference cost. `[FORUM-SIGNAL]` — widespread but not formally derived.

terms.short_def: Kaplan's combined form: $L(N, D) = [(N_c/N)^{\alpha_N/\alpha_D} + D_c/D]^{\alpha_D}$ `[kaplan2020 §1.2 Eq.(1.5); kb/excerpts/kaplan2020#sec…
terms.full_def: Kaplan's combined form: $L(N, D) = [(N_c/N)^{\alpha_N/\alpha_D} + D_c/D]^{\alpha_D}$ `[kaplan2020 §1.2 Eq.(1.5); kb/excerpts/kaplan2020#sec-1-2-joint]`. Implies sublinear $D \propto N^{0.74}$ to avoid overfitting penalty (in Kaplan's setup).

terms.short_def: Parameter count excluding token-embedding and positional-embedding tables.
terms.full_def: Parameter count excluding token-embedding and positional-embedding tables. Kaplan's $N$ uses this convention; Chinchilla and most modern papers use total parameters `[kaplan2020 §1.3; kb/excerpts/kaplan2020#sec-1-3-notation]`. The difference is ≈ 0.5%–5% at modern $d_{\text{model}}$ + vocab sizes.

terms.short_def: Compute spent during model inference (forward passes, search, verifier scoring) on a single query.
terms.full_def: Compute spent during model inference (forward passes, search, verifier scoring) on a single query. Distinct from training compute. Key paper: `snell2024`.

terms.short_def: The argmax over test-time hyperparameters $\theta$ of expected output correctness, at a fixed test-time compute budget $N$, conditioned on…
terms.full_def: The argmax over test-time hyperparameters $\theta$ of expected output correctness, at a fixed test-time compute budget $N$, conditioned on a prompt $q$: $\theta^\ast_{q, y^\ast(q)}(N) = \arg\max_\theta \mathbb{E}_{y \sim \mathrm{Target}(\theta, N, q)}[\mathbb{1}_{y = y^\ast(q)}]$

terms.short_def: The unified framing of test-time compute strategies as either (1) modifying the model's proposal distribution (CoT, revision, self-critique…
terms.full_def: The unified framing of test-time compute strategies as either (1) modifying the model's proposal distribution (CoT, revision, self-critique) or (2) applying a verifier (best-of-N, beam search, tree search) to selected candidates

terms.short_def: Generate $N$ independent candidate solutions, select the highest-scoring under an ORM.
terms.full_def: Generate $N$ independent candidate solutions, select the highest-scoring under an ORM. Baseline test-time-compute strategy. Snell's "best-of-N weighted" variant aggregates per-step PRM scores instead.

terms.short_def: Search over per-step PRM scores: at each step keep top $N/M$ candidates, expand each by $M$ proposals, repeat.
terms.full_def: Search over per-step PRM scores: at each step keep top $N/M$ candidates, expand each by $M$ proposals, repeat. $N$ = beam width, $M$ = expansion factor

terms.short_def: Beam search variant where each candidate's score includes simulated rollout of further steps before being kept/dropped.
terms.full_def: Beam search variant where each candidate's score includes simulated rollout of further steps before being kept/dropped. Costlier per beam, more discriminative.

terms.short_def: A 5-quantile bin of a base LLM's pass@1 rate (estimated from 2048 samples) on a test-set prompt.
terms.full_def: A 5-quantile bin of a base LLM's pass@1 rate (estimated from 2048 samples) on a test-set prompt. Used as a sufficient statistic for picking the compute-optimal test-time strategy

terms.short_def: Two operational forms of question-difficulty estimation: oracle uses ground-truth correctness (test-time only as research proxy); model-pre…
terms.full_def: Two operational forms of question-difficulty estimation: oracle uses ground-truth correctness (test-time only as research proxy); model-predicted uses a verifier's averaged score (deployable)

terms.short_def: A productionized API parameter (Gemini 2.5, OpenAI o-series reasoning-effort levels, Anthropic extended-thinking) that caps the test-time-c…
terms.full_def: A productionized API parameter (Gemini 2.5, OpenAI o-series reasoning-effort levels, Anthropic extended-thinking) that caps the test-time-compute spend per request. Operationalizes Snell's compute-optimal strategy

terms.short_def: An LLM trained (typically with RL on verifiable rewards) to produce long-form chain-of-thought before its final answer.
terms.full_def: An LLM trained (typically with RL on verifiable rewards) to produce long-form chain-of-thought before its final answer. o1, DeepSeek-R1, Qwen3-thinking-mode, Gemini 2.5-thinking-mode are exemplars

terms.short_def: Sample $N$ chain-of-thoughts independently at $T > 0$; majority-vote the final answers.
terms.full_def: Sample $N$ chain-of-thoughts independently at $T > 0$; majority-vote the final answers. Wang et al. 2022. Cheap baseline that requires no verifier.

terms.short_def: The unique-up-to-redundancy parametrization (init scales × LR scalings × parameter multipliers) under which optimal hyperparameters are wid…
terms.full_def: The unique-up-to-redundancy parametrization (init scales × LR scalings × parameter multipliers) under which optimal hyperparameters are width-invariant. The infinite-width limit has $\Theta(1)$-magnitude feature updates, in contrast to the kernel/NTK regime of standard parametrization

terms.short_def: The default parametrization in PyTorch and most frameworks: init variance $1/\text{fan\_in}$, uniform LR scaling.
terms.full_def: The default parametrization in PyTorch and most frameworks: init variance $1/\text{fan\_in}$, uniform LR scaling. Does NOT admit HP transfer across width — optimal LR shifts by ~1 order of magnitude as width grows from 256 to 8192

terms.short_def: The HP-tuning procedure: parametrize target in μP, tune HPs on a small proxy model (typically 1/16–1/32 of target width), copy them zero-sh…
terms.full_def: The HP-tuning procedure: parametrize target in μP, tune HPs on a small proxy model (typically 1/16–1/32 of target width), copy them zero-shot to target without retuning. Tuning cost ≈ 7% of one full pretraining run for GPT-3 6.7B

terms.short_def: Yang's framework characterizing a parametrization by three width-dependent functions per parameter: $a_W$ (parameter multiplier), $b_W$ (in…
terms.full_def: Yang's framework characterizing a parametrization by three width-dependent functions per parameter: $a_W$ (parameter multiplier), $b_W$ (init scale), $c_W$ (LR scaling). μP is a specific point in abc-space; SP is another. See `yang2022-mup` Appendices.

terms.short_def: In μTransfer, the small model used for HP search (proxy) and the full-scale model receiving the transferred HPs (target).
terms.full_def: In μTransfer, the small model used for HP search (proxy) and the full-scale model receiving the transferred HPs (target). Same depth, vocab, data, tokenizer; only width differs

terms.short_def: Definition 4.1 of Yang et al. 2022: replace Vaswani's $1/\sqrt{d_k}$ softmax scaling with $1/d_k$.
terms.full_def: Definition 4.1 of Yang et al. 2022: replace Vaswani's $1/\sqrt{d_k}$ softmax scaling with $1/d_k$. Correct training-time rescaling under μP because $q$ and $k$ become correlated after one optimization step, making $\mathrm{Var}(q^\top k) = \Theta(d)$

terms.short_def: μP combined with unit-scaling so every layer has predictable $\Theta(1)$-magnitude activations.
terms.full_def: μP combined with unit-scaling so every layer has predictable $\Theta(1)$-magnitude activations. Enables native FP8 training without dynamic per-tensor rescaling

terms.short_def: Tensor Programs VI extension of μP to the depth axis: principled depth-scaling HP transfer for pre-LN Transformers.
terms.full_def: Tensor Programs VI extension of μP to the depth axis: principled depth-scaling HP transfer for pre-LN Transformers. ICLR 2024.

terms.short_def: Meta's name for their cross-scale HP-transfer approach in the Llama 4 tech report `[meta-llama4]`.
terms.full_def: Meta's name for their cross-scale HP-transfer approach in the Llama 4 tech report `[meta-llama4]`. Details thin in the report; community working assumption is "μP-inspired."

terms.short_def: Two qualitatively different infinite-width limits: in the kernel/NTK regime, weights barely move during training (effectively a fixed kerne…
terms.full_def: Two qualitatively different infinite-width limits: in the kernel/NTK regime, weights barely move during training (effectively a fixed kernel machine); in the feature-learning regime, weights update by $\Theta(1)$ in the appropriate norm. μP is the unique parametrization (up to redundancy) in the latter regime. See Yang & Hu (Tensor Programs IV, 2021).

terms.short_def: Adam with decoupled weight decay.
terms.full_def: Adam with decoupled weight decay. Maintains per-parameter first and second moments $\mathbf{m}_t, \mathbf{v}_t$; update is $-\eta \cdot \mathbf{m}_t / (\sqrt{\mathbf{v}_t}+\varepsilon) - \eta\lambda\mathbf{w}_t$. The default LLM pre-training optimizer 2020-2024.

terms.short_def: "MomentUm Orthogonalized via Newton-Schulz." Replaces Adam's per-parameter rescaling with a *per-shape* matrix-orthogonalization on momentu…
terms.full_def: "MomentUm Orthogonalized via Newton-Schulz." Replaces Adam's per-parameter rescaling with a *per-shape* matrix-orthogonalization on momentum buffer $\mathbf{B}_t = \mu \mathbf{B}_{t-1} + \mathbf{G}_t$, then sets $\mathbf{O}_t = \text{Newton-Schulz}(\mathbf{B}_t)$ before the parameter update. Operates only on 2D weight matrices; uses AdamW for 1D parameters and embeddings.

terms.short_def: Iterative polynomial $\mathbf{X}_k = a\mathbf{X}_{k-1} + b(\mathbf{X}_{k-1}\mathbf{X}_{k-1}^\top)\mathbf{X}_{k-1} + c(\mathbf{X}_{k-1}\math…
terms.full_def: Iterative polynomial $\mathbf{X}_k = a\mathbf{X}_{k-1} + b(\mathbf{X}_{k-1}\mathbf{X}_{k-1}^\top)\mathbf{X}_{k-1} + c(\mathbf{X}_{k-1}\mathbf{X}_{k-1}^\top)^2 \mathbf{X}_{k-1}$ that converges all singular values of $\mathbf{X}_0$ to 1, producing $\mathbf{X}_0(\mathbf{X}_0^\top \mathbf{X}_0)^{-1/2}$. Muon uses 5 iterations with coefficients $(a, b, c) = (3.4445, -4.7750, 2.0315)$.

terms.short_def: Multiplier $0.2 \cdot \sqrt{\max(A, B)}$ applied to the orthogonalized update for an $A\times B$ weight matrix, equalizing update RMS acros…
terms.full_def: Multiplier $0.2 \cdot \sqrt{\max(A, B)}$ applied to the orthogonalized update for an $A\times B$ weight matrix, equalizing update RMS across heterogeneous matrix shapes.

terms.short_def: Matrix norm $\|\mathbf{M}\|_p^{(s)} = (\sum_i \sigma_i^p)^{1/p}$ on singular values.
terms.full_def: Matrix norm $\|\mathbf{M}\|_p^{(s)} = (\sum_i \sigma_i^p)^{1/p}$ on singular values. Muon's update is the steepest-descent direction under the Schatten-$\infty$ (spectral) norm constraint, which is why orthogonalization is the right pre-processing step.

terms.short_def: "Evolved sign momentum." Lighter optimizer using only sign of momentum: $\mathbf{w}_t \leftarrow \mathbf{w}_{t-1} - \eta \cdot \text{sign}(…
terms.full_def: "Evolved sign momentum." Lighter optimizer using only sign of momentum: $\mathbf{w}_t \leftarrow \mathbf{w}_{t-1} - \eta \cdot \text{sign}(\mu \mathbf{m}_{t-1} + (1-\mu)\mathbf{g}_t) - \eta\lambda\mathbf{w}_{t-1}$. Halves optimizer state vs. Adam. (Chen et al. 2023)

terms.short_def: "Second-order clipped stochastic optimization." Diagonal-Hessian preconditioning with clipping.
terms.full_def: "Second-order clipped stochastic optimization." Diagonal-Hessian preconditioning with clipping. Targets faster convergence in early pre-training. (Liu et al. 2023, arXiv 2305.14342)

terms.short_def: Learning-rate decay $\eta_t = \eta_{\min} + \tfrac{1}{2}(\eta_{\max}-\eta_{\min})(1 + \cos(\pi t / T))$, peaking after warmup and decaying…
terms.full_def: Learning-rate decay $\eta_t = \eta_{\min} + \tfrac{1}{2}(\eta_{\max}-\eta_{\min})(1 + \cos(\pi t / T))$, peaking after warmup and decaying to $\eta_{\min}$ at training end. The de-facto LLM schedule 2020-2024.

terms.short_def: Three-phase LR: linear warmup, then constant at $\eta_{\max}$ for the bulk of training, then short cooldown to ~$0.1\eta_{\max}$.
terms.full_def: Three-phase LR: linear warmup, then constant at $\eta_{\max}$ for the bulk of training, then short cooldown to ~$0.1\eta_{\max}$. Used by Moonlight (5.7T-token Muon run); avoids cosine's commitment to a specific budget.

terms.short_def: Open monthly web crawl run by the Common Crawl Foundation since 2008.
terms.full_def: Open monthly web crawl run by the Common Crawl Foundation since 2008. Source corpus for FineWeb (96 snapshots), C4, RefinedWeb, and most open web datasets. WARC format with extracted WET (text) variant.

terms.short_def: Python text-extraction library; extracts main article text from raw HTML.
terms.full_def: Python text-extraction library; extracts main article text from raw HTML. FineWeb's choice over CC's built-in WET, yielding ~25 % more text.

terms.short_def: Locality-sensitive hashing for near-duplicate detection: $n$-gram-shingle the document, sample $H$ hash-of-min-hash signatures arranged as…
terms.full_def: Locality-sensitive hashing for near-duplicate detection: $n$-gram-shingle the document, sample $H$ hash-of-min-hash signatures arranged as $B$ buckets of $R$ hashes ($H = BR$); two documents collide if any bucket matches; threshold Jaccard $\approx 1 - (1-r^R)^B$ where $r$ is true Jaccard. FineWeb uses 5-grams, $H=112$, $B=14$, $R=8$.

terms.short_def: FineWeb's choice to MinHash within each CC dump and not across dumps.
terms.full_def: FineWeb's choice to MinHash within each CC dump and not across dumps. Counterintuitively beats global dedup; the global version over-removes high-quality multi-snapshot text.

terms.short_def: Small reference model (1.71 B params, ~28 B tokens) trained on a candidate corpus to score it.
terms.full_def: Small reference model (1.71 B params, ~28 B tokens) trained on a candidate corpus to score it. Decisions are made by signed-difference of downstream-task accuracy across candidates.

terms.short_def: Per-line heuristics from Raffel et al. 2019 (T5/C4): remove lines lacking terminal punctuation, lines with "javascript", policy/cookie boil…
terms.full_def: Per-line heuristics from Raffel et al. 2019 (T5/C4): remove lines lacking terminal punctuation, lines with "javascript", policy/cookie boilerplate, etc. FineWeb adopts these *minus* the curly-brace rule (which would strip code).

terms.short_def: Three quantile-derived rules: lines-ending-with-terminal-punctuation $\leq 0.12$, char-duplication $\geq 0.01$, short-line-fraction $\geq 0…
terms.full_def: Three quantile-derived rules: lines-ending-with-terminal-punctuation $\leq 0.12$, char-duplication $\geq 0.01$, short-line-fraction $\geq 0.67$. Lift HellaSwag ~1 pt at ablation scale.

terms.short_def: 1.3 T-token quality-classifier-filtered subset of FineWeb.
terms.full_def: 1.3 T-token quality-classifier-filtered subset of FineWeb. Llama-3-70B-Instruct annotates 500 K samples 0–5 for "educational value"; classifier learns the score; threshold ≥3 keeps the subset. ~6 K H100-hours of annotation cost.

terms.short_def: $L_i(\mathbf{r}) = c_i + k_i \exp(\sum_j t_{ij} r_j)$.
terms.full_def: $L_i(\mathbf{r}) = c_i + k_i \exp(\sum_j t_{ij} r_j)$. Validation loss on domain $i$ as an exponential of a linear combination of training-mixture proportions $r_j$. Coefficients $(c_i, k_i, t_{ij})$ are fit per target domain.

terms.short_def: Pipeline that fits (training-step power law) inside (model-size power law) inside (mixing-law exponential), enabling small-model small-step…
terms.full_def: Pipeline that fits (training-step power law) inside (model-size power law) inside (mixing-law exponential), enabling small-model small-step experiments to predict large-model end-of-training loss as a function of mixture.

terms.short_def: The two structural constraints that pin down Eq. 1 from a candidate-function space: reduces to two-domain log-linear when $M=2$; invariant…
terms.full_def: The two structural constraints that pin down Eq. 1 from a candidate-function space: reduces to two-domain log-linear when $M=2$; invariant under re-labeling of domains.

terms.short_def: Fitted parameter in the data mixing law; sign indicates whether domain-$j$ training helps ($t_{ij}<0$) or hurts ($t_{ij}>0$) validation on…
terms.full_def: Fitted parameter in the data mixing law; sign indicates whether domain-$j$ training helps ($t_{ij}<0$) or hurts ($t_{ij}>0$) validation on domain $i$.

terms.short_def: Documents generated by a teacher LLM (or LLM pipeline) for use in a student's pre-training mix.
terms.full_def: Documents generated by a teacher LLM (or LLM pipeline) for use in a student's pre-training mix. Phi-4 generated ~400 B tokens via 50 pipelines, ≈40 % of its 9.8 T-token training mix.

terms.short_def: Multi-stage pipeline: seed curation → rewrite/augment → self-revision → quality filter.
terms.full_def: Multi-stage pipeline: seed curation → rewrite/augment → self-revision → quality filter. 50 distinct pipelines tuned for different content types (STEM, code, reasoning chains).

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-R1's 800 K SFT samples (600 K reasoning + 200 K non-reasoning) generated by R1 and used to fine-tune Qwen-2.5/Llama-3 students at…
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-R1's 800 K SFT samples (600 K reasoning + 200 K non-reasoning) generated by R1 and used to fine-tune Qwen-2.5/Llama-3 students at 1.5/7/14/32/70 B. Transfers reasoning *capability* to architectures that cannot reach the same level via RLVR alone.

terms.short_def: Held-out test sets, especially November 2024 AMC math contests and unleaked HumanEval+, used to verify the synthetic data did not leak benc…
terms.full_def: Held-out test sets, especially November 2024 AMC math contests and unleaked HumanEval+, used to verify the synthetic data did not leak benchmark answers via the teacher.

terms.short_def: `[INTUITION]` Synthetic data presents *concept-then-application-then-explanation* as a self-contained unit, vs. organic web data which scat…
terms.full_def: `[INTUITION]` Synthetic data presents *concept-then-application-then-explanation* as a self-contained unit, vs. organic web data which scatters those parts across many documents. Enables high-density learning per token.

terms.short_def: Same parameters on each worker; each worker processes a shard of the global batch; gradients all-reduced before optimizer step.
terms.full_def: Same parameters on each worker; each worker processes a shard of the global batch; gradients all-reduced before optimizer step.

terms.short_def: "Fully Sharded Data Parallel." Native PyTorch ZeRO implementation.
terms.full_def: "Fully Sharded Data Parallel." Native PyTorch ZeRO implementation. Parameters and optimizer state sharded across DP workers; full param-set materialized via all-gather just before each forward/backward.

terms.short_def: Per-parameter `DTensor`-sharded successor to FSDP1.
terms.full_def: Per-parameter `DTensor`-sharded successor to FSDP1. ~7 % per-GPU memory reduction and ~1.5 % throughput improvement vs. FSDP1's flat-parameter design.

terms.short_def: Optimizer-state / gradient / parameter sharding levels of Rajbhandari et al.'s Zero-Redundancy Optimizer.
terms.full_def: Optimizer-state / gradient / parameter sharding levels of Rajbhandari et al.'s Zero-Redundancy Optimizer. ZeRO-1 shards only optimizer state; ZeRO-3 shards everything (FSDP's default).

terms.short_def: Within-layer matmul split (column-parallel `Y = XA`, row-parallel `Z = YB`).
terms.full_def: Within-layer matmul split (column-parallel `Y = XA`, row-parallel `Z = YB`). Inserts one all-reduce per matmul. Each worker holds a $1/t$ slice of weights.

terms.short_def: Companion to TP: shards normalization and dropout layers along the sequence dimension to reduce activation memory.
terms.full_def: Companion to TP: shards normalization and dropout layers along the sequence dimension to reduce activation memory.

terms.short_def: Stage-level layer split.
terms.full_def: Stage-level layer split. Microbatches flow stage-to-stage via P2P send/recv. Tradeoff: per-GPU param drops by $1/p$ but in-flight activations grow.

terms.short_def: Classic PP schedule.
terms.full_def: Classic PP schedule. Each stage alternates forward-of-microbatch-$i$ with backward-of-microbatch-$j$ to bound peak activation memory.

terms.short_def: Fraction of PP step time where some stage is idle, waiting for upstream/downstream.
terms.full_def: Fraction of PP step time where some stage is idle, waiting for upstream/downstream. 1F1B bubble is $(p-1)(F+B)$ stage-times.

terms.short_def: PP schedule that splits backward into "backward-for-input" ($B$) and "backward-for-weights" ($W$); reorders $W$ into the bubble.
terms.full_def: PP schedule that splits backward into "backward-for-input" ($B$) and "backward-for-weights" ($W$); reorders $W$ into the bubble. Reduces bubble to $(p-1)(F+B-2W)$.

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-V3's bidirectional PP.
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-V3's bidirectional PP. Microbatches feed from both ends of the pipeline simultaneously; each chunk split into 4 stages (attention / dispatch / MLP / combine), backward further split via ZB; achieves $(\tfrac{p}{2}-1)(F\&B+B-3W)$ bubble at $2\times$ parameter memory and $+1$ activation.

terms.short_def: Token-position sharding of the sequence dimension.
terms.full_def: Token-position sharding of the sequence dimension. Communication-pattern resembles ring attention. Enables 262 144-token training of Llama 3.1 8B on 8× H100.

terms.short_def: MoE-specific sharding: each worker hosts a subset of the experts.
terms.full_def: MoE-specific sharding: each worker hosts a subset of the experts. Forward pass requires an all-to-all dispatch (each token to its top-$k$ experts) and a combine all-to-all. DeepSeek-V3 uses $e=64$ across 8 nodes.

terms.short_def: The two collectives bracketing every MoE forward.
terms.full_def: The two collectives bracketing every MoE forward. Dispatch routes tokens to expert hosts; combine returns expert outputs to original token positions. The dominant communication cost in MoE training.

terms.short_def: Fractionalizes TP matmuls into chunks and overlaps each chunk's communication with the previous chunk's compute via intra-node `SymmetricMe…
terms.full_def: Fractionalizes TP matmuls into chunks and overlaps each chunk's communication with the previous chunk's compute via intra-node `SymmetricMemory` shared buffers.

terms.short_def: Distributed tensor abstraction; weights are stored as DTensor with a placement spec on a $d \times t \times p \times c \times e$ device mes…
terms.full_def: Distributed tensor abstraction; weights are stored as DTensor with a placement spec on a $d \times t \times p \times c \times e$ device mesh. The composability layer for 4D/5D parallelism.

terms.short_def: Each token's MoE dispatch is limited to at most 4 nodes, exploiting the 3.2× NVLink/IB bandwidth ratio: one IB hop per (token, target-node)…
terms.full_def: Each token's MoE dispatch is limited to at most 4 nodes, exploiting the 3.2× NVLink/IB bandwidth ratio: one IB hop per (token, target-node) followed by intra-node NVLink forwarding.

terms.short_def: Realized training throughput as a fraction of peak hardware FLOPS.
terms.full_def: Realized training throughput as a fraction of peak hardware FLOPS. Standard parallelism-plan quality metric.

terms.short_def: Brain-float.
terms.full_def: Brain-float. 8-bit exponent, 7-bit mantissa (vs. FP16's 5-exp/10-mant). Same dynamic range as FP32; lower precision. The 2020-2024 default forward/backward dtype.

terms.short_def: 4-bit exponent, 3-bit mantissa.
terms.full_def: 4-bit exponent, 3-bit mantissa. Higher precision, lower dynamic range than E5M2. DeepSeek-V3 uses E4M3 on *all* tensors via fine-grained scaling.

terms.short_def: 5-bit exponent, 2-bit mantissa.
terms.full_def: 5-bit exponent, 2-bit mantissa. Higher dynamic range, lower precision. NVIDIA's recommended dtype for `Dgrad`/`Wgrad` in their hybrid recipe; DeepSeek-V3 *avoids* it.

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-V3's activation scaling: one FP8 scale per (token, 128-channel block) tile.
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-V3's activation scaling: one FP8 scale per (token, 128-channel block) tile. Adapts to per-token outliers.

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-V3's weight scaling: one FP8 scale per 128×128 weight block.
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-V3's weight scaling: one FP8 scale per 128×128 weight block.

terms.short_def: DeepSeek-V3's accumulation strategy: every $N_C=128$ MMA steps, copy partial sums from Tensor-Core (~14-bit) accumulator to FP32 registers…
terms.full_def: DeepSeek-V3's accumulation strategy: every $N_C=128$ MMA steps, copy partial sums from Tensor-Core (~14-bit) accumulator to FP32 registers on CUDA cores for full-precision summation. Recovers ~2 % systematic error in raw FP8 GEMM.

terms.short_def: Components held at BF16/FP32 even with FP8 elsewhere: embedding module, output head, MoE gating, normalization, attention.
terms.full_def: Components held at BF16/FP32 even with FP8 elsewhere: embedding module, output head, MoE gating, normalization, attention.

terms.short_def: Sudden non-recoverable loss-curve excursion.
terms.full_def: Sudden non-recoverable loss-curve excursion. Causes include gradient-norm explosion, attention-logit divergence, embedding-norm growth, and FP8 underflow on rare tokens. DeepSeek-V3 reports zero such spikes in 14.8 T tokens.

terms.short_def: Continuing training of an already-trained model on a different (typically higher-quality or domain-specific) data mixture.
terms.full_def: Continuing training of an already-trained model on a different (typically higher-quality or domain-specific) data mixture. Phi-4's "midtrain," DeepSeek-V3's 119 K-H800-hour context-extension stage, OLMo 2's Dolmino mix.

terms.short_def: Sub-stage of late-stage pre-training that increases trained sequence length (e.g., 4 K → 32 K → 128 K) by adjusting RoPE scaling and contin…
terms.full_def: Sub-stage of late-stage pre-training that increases trained sequence length (e.g., 4 K → 32 K → 128 K) by adjusting RoPE scaling and continuing on long-context data.

terms.short_def: Family of fine-tuning methods that update only a small fraction of parameters: LoRA, DoRA, IA3, prefix-tuning.
terms.full_def: Family of fine-tuning methods that update only a small fraction of parameters: LoRA, DoRA, IA3, prefix-tuning. Pending excerpts.

terms.short_def: Combining trained models via parameter-level operations (averaging, interpolation, sign-resolution, sparsification).
terms.full_def: Combining trained models via parameter-level operations (averaging, interpolation, sign-resolution, sparsification). Subtypes include weight averaging (Model Soups), task arithmetic, TIES, DARE, evolutionary merging. Pending excerpts.

terms.short_def: The empirical finding that fine-tunes of a shared base land in a connected loss basin: averaging their weights gives a model with loss simi…
terms.full_def: The empirical finding that fine-tunes of a shared base land in a connected loss basin: averaging their weights gives a model with loss similar to the endpoints. The leading explanation for why merging works at all. (Frankle, Dziugaite et al. 2020 — pending excerpt.)

terms.short_def: A high-precision (FP32) copy of the model parameters maintained alongside a low-precision (FP16/BF16/FP8) working copy used in the forward/…
terms.full_def: A high-precision (FP32) copy of the model parameters maintained alongside a low-precision (FP16/BF16/FP8) working copy used in the forward/backward pass. Optimizer updates are applied to the master copy and cast down to the working copy.

terms.short_def: Multiplying the loss by a scale factor $S$ before backprop, then dividing the resulting gradients by $S$ before the master-weight update.
terms.full_def: Multiplying the loss by a scale factor $S$ before backprop, then dividing the resulting gradients by $S$ before the master-weight update. Shifts the gradient distribution upward in the FP16 representable range to avoid underflow.

terms.short_def: A 16-bit floating-point format with 8 exponent bits and 7 mantissa bits.
terms.full_def: A 16-bit floating-point format with 8 exponent bits and 7 mantissa bits. Same dynamic range as FP32; half the precision of FP16. The universal LLM pre-training default by 2023.
