terms.short_def: 8-bit float formats with 4-bit exponent + 3-bit mantissa (E4M3, range $\pm 448$) or 5-bit exponent + 2-bit mantissa (E5M2, larger range, lo…
terms.full_def: 8-bit float formats with 4-bit exponent + 3-bit mantissa (E4M3, range $\pm 448$) or 5-bit exponent + 2-bit mantissa (E5M2, larger range, lower precision). NVIDIA's hybrid recipe uses E4M3 forward and E5M2 backward; DeepSeek-V3 uses E4M3 everywhere with fine-grained tile scaling.

terms.short_def: Per-group scaling factors along inner dimensions of a GEMM, allowing each tile/block to choose its own scale and absorb local outliers.
terms.full_def: Per-group scaling factors along inner dimensions of a GEMM, allowing each tile/block to choose its own scale and absorb local outliers. DeepSeek-V3's 1×128 (activations) and 128×128 (weights) scheme.

terms.short_def: Periodic (every $N_C = 128$ elements) copy of FP8 Tensor Core partial sums to FP32 registers on CUDA cores, where full-precision accumulati…
terms.full_def: Periodic (every $N_C = 128$ elements) copy of FP8 Tensor Core partial sums to FP32 registers on CUDA cores, where full-precision accumulation is performed. Recovers accumulation precision lost to Tensor Core's 14-bit internal accumulator.

terms.short_def: 4-bit floating-point format (E2M1) with native hardware MXScale (32-element per-block scaling) on Blackwell-class GPUs.
terms.full_def: 4-bit floating-point format (E2M1) with native hardware MXScale (32-element per-block scaling) on Blackwell-class GPUs. Production-stability for full pre-training is open as of 2026-05.

terms.short_def: A sudden upward jump in pre-training loss, often caused by gradient-norm explosion, attention-logit divergence, embedding-norm growth, or F…
terms.full_def: A sudden upward jump in pre-training loss, often caused by gradient-norm explosion, attention-logit divergence, embedding-norm growth, or FP8 underflow. May or may not recover. DeepSeek-V3 reports zero irrecoverable spikes over 14.8T tokens

terms.short_def: Stability machinery to prevent attention-logit blow-up: normalize $Q$ and $K$ before the dot product (QK-LayerNorm, Henry et al. 2020) or c…
terms.full_def: Stability machinery to prevent attention-logit blow-up: normalize $Q$ and $K$ before the dot product (QK-LayerNorm, Henry et al. 2020) or clamp $QK^\top$ before softmax (QK-clip).

terms.short_def: Parameterizes a frozen weight matrix's update as a rank-$r$ product $BA$ with $r \ll \min(d_{\text{in}}, d_{\text{out}})$; only $A, B$ are…
terms.full_def: Parameterizes a frozen weight matrix's update as a rank-$r$ product $BA$ with $r \ll \min(d_{\text{in}}, d_{\text{out}})$; only $A, B$ are trained. Merges to a single weight at inference time with no latency cost.

terms.short_def: LoRA over a 4-bit-quantized (NF4) base model.
terms.full_def: LoRA over a 4-bit-quantized (NF4) base model. Adds double-quantization and paged optimizers. 65B fine-tune on a single 48GB GPU.

terms.short_def: 4-bit quantization with quantile-spaced bin centers matched to a standard normal distribution.
terms.full_def: 4-bit quantization with quantile-spaced bin centers matched to a standard normal distribution. Information- theoretically near-optimal for pretrained weights, which are empirically near-Gaussian per block.

terms.short_def: LoRA variant decomposing $W$ into magnitude and direction; LoRA the direction only.
terms.full_def: LoRA variant decomposing $W$ into magnitude and direction; LoRA the direction only. Cited via Liu et al. 2024. Often outperforms LoRA at similar parameter count.

terms.short_def: A direction in weight space defined as $\tau_T = \theta_T - \theta_0$ where $\theta_0$ is a pretrained base and $\theta_T$ is the same base…
terms.full_def: A direction in weight space defined as $\tau_T = \theta_T - \theta_0$ where $\theta_0$ is a pretrained base and $\theta_T$ is the same base fine-tuned on task $T$. Arithmetic on task vectors (addition, negation, combination) steers model behavior.

terms.short_def: A model produced by averaging the weights of $K$ fine-tunes from a shared base.
terms.full_def: A model produced by averaging the weights of $K$ fine-tunes from a shared base. *Uniform soup* averages all; *greedy soup* iteratively includes only fine-tunes that don't decrease validation accuracy.

terms.short_def: Three-step model merge: trim each task vector to top-$k\%$, elect a sign per coordinate by magnitude-weighted majority, disjoint-merge the…
terms.full_def: Three-step model merge: trim each task vector to top-$k\%$, elect a sign per coordinate by magnitude-weighted majority, disjoint-merge the surviving same-sign entries.

terms.short_def: Bernoulli-drop $1-p$ of task-vector entries and rescale survivors by $1/p$ before TIES or Soup merging.
terms.full_def: Bernoulli-drop $1-p$ of task-vector entries and rescale survivors by $1/p$ before TIES or Soup merging. Yu et al. 2024.

terms.short_def: Open-source toolkit for model merging supporting Linear / SLERP / TIES / DARE / passthrough merges with per-tensor configuration.
terms.full_def: Open-source toolkit for model merging supporting Linear / SLERP / TIES / DARE / passthrough merges with per-tensor configuration.

terms.short_def: The empirical property that fine-tunes of a shared base land in a connected loss basin: the path $\theta_0 + t(\theta_f - \theta_0)$, $t \i…
terms.full_def: The empirical property that fine-tunes of a shared base land in a connected loss basin: the path $\theta_0 + t(\theta_f - \theta_0)$, $t \in [0,1]$, stays in low-loss territory. Underpins why model merging works.

terms.short_def: Layer-wise interleaving of layers from two or more models (e.g., copy layers 0–15 from model A and 16–31 from model B).
terms.full_def: Layer-wise interleaving of layers from two or more models (e.g., copy layers 0–15 from model A and 16–31 from model B). Widely used in OSS practice; limited primary literature. [FORUM-SIGNAL].

terms.short_def: Setting per-token loss weight to 0 on prompt tokens and 1 on response tokens, so only response tokens contribute gradient.
terms.full_def: Setting per-token loss weight to 0 on prompt tokens and 1 on response tokens, so only response tokens contribute gradient. The standard convention; multi-turn variants train on all assistant turns or only the last.

terms.short_def: Sample $N$ responses per prompt from the current policy, score with a reward model, keep the top-$k$, and SFT on those.
terms.full_def: Sample $N$ responses per prompt from the current policy, score with a reward model, keep the top-$k$, and SFT on those. The Llama-3 quality gate before each preference round.

terms.short_def: Procedure exploiting that an aligned LM, prompted with only its instruction- template prefix (no question), will fabricate the question and…
terms.full_def: Procedure exploiting that an aligned LM, prompted with only its instruction- template prefix (no question), will fabricate the question and then answer it. Produces $(x,y)$ SFT pairs at zero human cost.

terms.short_def: Fine-tuning a small base model on $(x,y)$ traces sampled from a strong RL-trained reasoner (e.g., R1-Distill: 800K samples from DeepSeek-R1…
terms.full_def: Fine-tuning a small base model on $(x,y)$ traces sampled from a strong RL-trained reasoner (e.g., R1-Distill: 800K samples from DeepSeek-R1; s1: 1K curated traces from Gemini Flash Thinking). Recovers most reasoning capability with no RL.

terms.short_def: Training a model to emit `<think>...</think>` blocks gated by a system prompt, switching reasoning behavior on/off.
terms.full_def: Training a model to emit `<think>...</think>` blocks gated by a system prompt, switching reasoning behavior on/off. Qwen 3's 4-stage post-training innovation.

terms.short_def: Original instruction-tuning recipe: ~1.8K NLP tasks reformatted as natural-language instructions, used as an SFT mixture.
terms.full_def: Original instruction-tuning recipe: ~1.8K NLP tasks reformatted as natural-language instructions, used as an SFT mixture. The pre-RLHF post-training baseline. `[wei2021-flan]`. FLAN-T5 `[chung2022-flan-t5]` and the FLAN Collection (Longpre et al. 2023, arXiv:2301.13688) extend it.

terms.short_def: RLHF pipeline where preference labels come from an AI feedback model rather than human labelers.
terms.full_def: RLHF pipeline where preference labels come from an AI feedback model rather than human labelers. The reward model is trained on AI-generated preferences via standard Bradley–Terry.

terms.short_def: A list of natural-language principles ("the assistant should not...") used to condition the AI feedback model's preferences.
terms.full_def: A list of natural-language principles ("the assistant should not...") used to condition the AI feedback model's preferences. The only human-authored alignment input in Constitutional AI.

terms.short_def: Stage 1 of Constitutional AI: sample a response from a helpful-only model, critique under a randomly-chosen principle, revise, and SFT on t…
terms.full_def: Stage 1 of Constitutional AI: sample a response from a helpful-only model, critique under a randomly-chosen principle, revise, and SFT on the revisions. Produces $\pi^{\mathrm{SL-CAI}}$.

terms.short_def: Stage 2 of Constitutional AI: sample response pairs from $\pi^{\mathrm{SL-CAI}}$, have an AI judge choose under a principle, train a reward…
terms.full_def: Stage 2 of Constitutional AI: sample response pairs from $\pi^{\mathrm{SL-CAI}}$, have an AI judge choose under a principle, train a reward model on AI preferences, run PPO with KL anchor.

terms.short_def: A model trained with CAI engages with harmful prompts by stating its objections, rather than refusing flatly.
terms.full_def: A model trained with CAI engages with harmful prompts by stating its objections, rather than refusing flatly. The defining behavioral signature distinguishing CAI from helpful-only RLHF (which complies) and from naive safety RLHF (which refuses without explanation).

terms.short_def: Inverts the constitution and applies probability-clamped RLAIF to generate controlled adversarial / toxic data.
terms.full_def: Inverts the constitution and applies probability-clamped RLAIF to generate controlled adversarial / toxic data.

terms.short_def: Using a previously-trained RLAIF model as the feedback model for the next round of RLAIF.
terms.full_def: Using a previously-trained RLAIF model as the feedback model for the next round of RLAIF. Risks model collapse in the constitution direction.

terms.short_def: Generation procedure that, at each step, masks the logits to allow only tokens consistent with a target grammar (regex, JSON schema, GBNF,…
terms.full_def: Generation procedure that, at each step, masks the logits to allow only tokens consistent with a target grammar (regex, JSON schema, GBNF, CFG). The sampling distribution is restricted to $\mathcal{V}_{\text{allowed}}(s_t)$, the FSM's allowed-token set in the current state.

terms.short_def: Precomputed mapping from each FSM state to the set of allowed vocabulary tokens.
terms.full_def: Precomputed mapping from each FSM state to the set of allowed vocabulary tokens. Built once per grammar; reduces per-step constraint check from $O(V)$ to amortized $O(1)$.

terms.short_def: Partition of the vocabulary by whether allowed/disallowed status depends only on the parser state (context-independent: precomputable bitma…
terms.full_def: Partition of the vocabulary by whether allowed/disallowed status depends only on the parser state (context-independent: precomputable bitmask) or also on stack contents (context-dependent: runtime check). XGrammar reports ~99% context-independent for typical JSON schemas.

terms.short_def: FSM transformation that merges chains of singular-transition edges into single edges, allowing multi-token decoding when the grammar determ…
terms.full_def: FSM transformation that merges chains of singular-transition edges into single edges, allowing multi-token decoding when the grammar deterministically forces a literal substring (e.g., `{"summary": "` in a JSON schema).

terms.short_def: llama.cpp's grammar specification format.
terms.full_def: llama.cpp's grammar specification format. An extended BNF used to define constrained-decoding grammars.

terms.short_def: Vendor-facing API features (OpenAI `response_format`, Anthropic tool-use schemas, etc.) that guarantee output conforms to a schema.
terms.full_def: Vendor-facing API features (OpenAI `response_format`, Anthropic tool-use schemas, etc.) that guarantee output conforms to a schema. Implemented underneath via FSM-constrained decoding. Exact-conformance guarantees depend on whether the backend uses true FSM or only logit-bias.

terms.short_def: Benchmark of 10K real-world JSON schemas used to compare constrained-decoding engines.
terms.full_def: Benchmark of 10K real-world JSON schemas used to compare constrained-decoding engines.

terms.short_def: A (typically linear) classifier $g: \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathcal{Y}$ trained on frozen LM activations $\mathbf{h}^{(\ell, t)}$ to predict an e…
terms.full_def: A (typically linear) classifier $g: \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathcal{Y}$ trained on frozen LM activations $\mathbf{h}^{(\ell, t)}$ to predict an external property $y$. The model's parameters are held fixed; only the probe is trained. Foundational to interpretability methodology

terms.short_def: Probe of the form $g(\mathbf{h}) = \sigma(\mathbf{w}^\top \mathbf{h} + b)$ with $\mathbf{w} \in \mathbb{R}^d$.
terms.full_def: Probe of the form $g(\mathbf{h}) = \sigma(\mathbf{w}^\top \mathbf{h} + b)$ with $\mathbf{w} \in \mathbb{R}^d$. Distinguished from non-linear / MLP probes that can suffer from "probe leakage" (learning the property from low-information features)

terms.short_def: Hewitt & Manning's geometric probe that predicts higher-arity structure (e.g., parse-tree distance) by learning a linear transformation $\m…
terms.full_def: Hewitt & Manning's geometric probe that predicts higher-arity structure (e.g., parse-tree distance) by learning a linear transformation $\mathbf{B} \in \mathbb{R}^{k \times d}$ such that $\|\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{h}_i - \mathbf{h}_j)\|_2^2 \approx d_T(w_i, w_j)$ for tokens $i, j$

terms.short_def: Parameter-free probe that defines the property direction as $\mathbf{d}_{\mathrm{MM}} = \boldsymbol{\mu}_+ - \boldsymbol{\mu}_-$ (per-class…
terms.full_def: Parameter-free probe that defines the property direction as $\mathbf{d}_{\mathrm{MM}} = \boldsymbol{\mu}_+ - \boldsymbol{\mu}_-$ (per-class mean difference). Closed-form, no optimization required. Marks & Tegmark 2023 argue the directions found are "more causally implicated in model outputs" than logistic-regression probe directions on the same data

terms.short_def: The argument (consolidated in Belinkov 2022) that high-accuracy probes are correlational and can read properties the model represents but d…
terms.full_def: The argument (consolidated in Belinkov 2022) that high-accuracy probes are correlational and can read properties the model represents but does not *use*. Three named confounders: probe-expressivity, spurious-correlation, vestigial-information

terms.short_def: The 2023+ pattern of pairing probes with activation interventions (direction ablation, addition, steering) to test whether the probe's dire…
terms.full_def: The 2023+ pattern of pairing probes with activation interventions (direction ablation, addition, steering) to test whether the probe's direction is *causally* used by the model, not merely correlated with the property. Marks & Tegmark 2023 demonstrate this on a truth direction in LLaMA-2-13B that flips TRUE/FALSE prediction when ablated

terms.short_def: Causal-probing intervention that projects a residual-stream activation onto the orthogonal complement of a probe direction $\hat{\mathbf{d}…
terms.full_def: Causal-probing intervention that projects a residual-stream activation onto the orthogonal complement of a probe direction $\hat{\mathbf{d}}$: $\mathbf{h} \mapsto \mathbf{h} - (\mathbf{h}^\top \hat{\mathbf{d}})\hat{\mathbf{d}}$, then runs the rest of the model. A behavior change validates causal use of $\hat{\mathbf{d}}$

terms.short_def: Inference-time intervention that adds $\alpha \hat{\mathbf{d}}$ to a residual-stream activation to amplify (or, with $\alpha < 0$, suppress…
terms.full_def: Inference-time intervention that adds $\alpha \hat{\mathbf{d}}$ to a residual-stream activation to amplify (or, with $\alpha < 0$, suppress) a probed direction. The Representation Engineering / Vennemeyer 2025 line

terms.short_def: A direction in residual-stream space whose projection sign correlates with (and, validated by Marks & Tegmark 2023, causally controls) the…
terms.full_def: A direction in residual-stream space whose projection sign correlates with (and, validated by Marks & Tegmark 2023, causally controls) the model's TRUE/FALSE prediction on factual statements. Localizes around layer 12-15 of LLaMA-2-13B at end-of-statement punctuation tokens

terms.short_def: The empirical pattern (Tigges et al. 2023, Marks & Tegmark 2023) that information about a clause is encoded over the clause-ending punctuat…
terms.full_def: The empirical pattern (Tigges et al. 2023, Marks & Tegmark 2023) that information about a clause is encoded over the clause-ending punctuation token, making period-position activations the canonical probing target for sentence-level properties

terms.short_def: 57-subject 4-way MCQ benchmark introduced by Hendrycks et al. 2020 / ICLR 2021.
terms.full_def: 57-subject 4-way MCQ benchmark introduced by Hendrycks et al. 2020 / ICLR 2021. 15,908 questions sourced from public exam banks (AP, GRE, MCAT, professional licensing). Foundational for the post-2021 LLM eval era; saturated at >90% on frontier models as of 2026

terms.short_def: Polo et al. 2024 audit of MMLU finding 6.49% baseline error rate, with the Virology subset reaching 57% errors.
terms.full_def: Polo et al. 2024 audit of MMLU finding 6.49% baseline error rate, with the Virology subset reaching 57% errors. Per-question corrections published; methodology audit, not a new benchmark

terms.short_def: 10-way MCQ, reasoning-augmented successor to MMLU.
terms.full_def: 10-way MCQ, reasoning-augmented successor to MMLU. 12,032 items, 16-33 percentage point drop in headline score relative to MMLU at launch, 2× lower prompt-format sensitivity

terms.short_def: 198-question subset of GPQA (Rein et al. 2023) where two PhD experts agree on the answer and at least one non-expert validator with web acc…
terms.full_def: 198-question subset of GPQA (Rein et al. 2023) where two PhD experts agree on the answer and at least one non-expert validator with web access got it wrong. The "Google-proof" property is operationalized via the 34% non-expert-with-web baseline. Saturated at frontier as of 2026 (>90%)

terms.short_def: The training-data-induced bias that pre-trained LMs do not have uniform priors over A/B/C/D in MCQ items.
terms.full_def: The training-data-induced bias that pre-trained LMs do not have uniform priors over A/B/C/D in MCQ items. Letter bias can favor one option by several percentage points; 10-way MCQ (MMLU-Pro) dilutes this effect

terms.short_def: On a knowledge-dominant benchmark (MMLU), Chain-of-Thought scoring gives roughly the same result as direct-answer scoring; on a reasoning-d…
terms.full_def: On a knowledge-dominant benchmark (MMLU), Chain-of-Thought scoring gives roughly the same result as direct-answer scoring; on a reasoning-dominant benchmark (MMLU-Pro), CoT improves the score measurably. The gap is a diagnostic for whether a benchmark requires multi-step inference

terms.short_def: A benchmark where items combine factual retrieval and multi-step reasoning.
terms.full_def: A benchmark where items combine factual retrieval and multi-step reasoning. MMLU-Pro, GPQA, HLE all qualify; the post-MMLU eval landscape has largely abandoned pure-knowledge benchmarks in favor of hybrids

terms.short_def: Headline metric in adversarial-robustness benchmarks: fraction of (behavior, attack) pairs for which the model produces an output the judge…
terms.full_def: Headline metric in adversarial-robustness benchmarks: fraction of (behavior, attack) pairs for which the model produces an output the judge rates harmful. Not directly comparable across papers unless the judge, behaviors, and attack family match

terms.short_def: Mazeika et al. 2024 standardized red-team evaluation framework: 510 behaviors in four functional categories, 18 attack methods, 33 target L…
terms.full_def: Mazeika et al. 2024 standardized red-team evaluation framework: 510 behaviors in four functional categories, 18 attack methods, 33 target LLMs/defenses. Used by AISI for pre-deployment evals

terms.short_def: Chao et al. 2024 reproducibility-focused jailbreak benchmark: 100 OpenAI-policy-aligned behaviors, evolving repository of jailbreak artifac…
terms.full_def: Chao et al. 2024 reproducibility-focused jailbreak benchmark: 100 OpenAI-policy-aligned behaviors, evolving repository of jailbreak artifacts (the actual successful prompts), standardized LLM-as-judge, public leaderboard. NeurIPS 2024 D&B

terms.short_def: Russinovich et al. 2024 attack: gradually escalate over multiple benign-seeming turns, exploiting the LM's tendency to over-weight its own…
terms.full_def: Russinovich et al. 2024 attack: gradually escalate over multiple benign-seeming turns, exploiting the LM's tendency to over-weight its own prior committed responses. Achieves 29-71% ASR uplift over single-turn baselines on AdvBench against GPT-4 / Gemini-Pro. Documents that single-turn evals systematically underestimate the attack surface

terms.short_def: The automated implementation of Crescendo: an attacker LLM generates the escalation chain.
terms.full_def: The automated implementation of Crescendo: an attacker LLM generates the escalation chain. The 29-71% ASR uplift number refers to Crescendomation specifically

terms.short_def: Wei, Haghtalab, Steinhardt 2023: when a model's capability training and safety training pull in different directions, attacks that invoke t…
terms.full_def: Wei, Haghtalab, Steinhardt 2023: when a model's capability training and safety training pull in different directions, attacks that invoke the capability override the safety training. One of two named failure modes

terms.short_def: Wei et al. 2023: safety training fails to generalize to a domain (Base64, low-resource language, role-play) where the model has capabilitie…
terms.full_def: Wei et al. 2023: safety training fails to generalize to a domain (Base64, low-resource language, role-play) where the model has capabilities. The other named failure mode

terms.short_def: Wei et al. 2023's policy recommendation: safety mechanisms must be as sophisticated as the model's full capability range.
terms.full_def: Wei et al. 2023's policy recommendation: safety mechanisms must be as sophisticated as the model's full capability range. Partial-domain safety training will be defeated by attacks that target the capability/safety gap

terms.short_def: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy tier framework.
terms.full_def: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy tier framework. ASL-1 (low risk, no autonomy) → ASL-2 (current frontier baseline) → ASL-3 (substantially elevated risk; specific deployment controls required) → ASL-4 (autonomous AI risk; not yet deployed). Each tier specifies capability-evaluation triggers and security/containment commitments

terms.short_def: Capability-evaluation metric framing model performance as the human-time-to-complete distribution at which the model succeeds at >50%.
terms.full_def: Capability-evaluation metric framing model performance as the human-time-to-complete distribution at which the model succeeds at >50%. Becoming the field's lingua franca for capability quantification across labs and external evaluators

terms.short_def: Pre-deployment eval that tests whether a model can autonomously carry out a task whose successful completion would constitute a threat (cyb…
terms.full_def: Pre-deployment eval that tests whether a model can autonomously carry out a task whose successful completion would constitute a threat (cyber CTFs, autonomous replication, biology / CBRN tasks). Inverts the usual "model refuses harm" frame to "model could *do* harm if it tried"

terms.short_def: Failure mode in safety evals: when the judge classifier is from the same model family as the model being evaluated, it may systematically u…
terms.full_def: Failure mode in safety evals: when the judge classifier is from the same model family as the model being evaluated, it may systematically underrate harmful generations from that family. Cross-judge agreement testing is the partial mitigation

terms.short_def: The fine-tuned classifier (Llama-2-13B family, fine-tuned on labeled harmful/non-harmful generations) shipped with HarmBench as the standar…
terms.full_def: The fine-tuned classifier (Llama-2-13B family, fine-tuned on labeled harmful/non-harmful generations) shipped with HarmBench as the standardized judge for the 510 behaviors

terms.short_def: Eval protocol that presents the model with a deployment-realistic context (or, conversely, a clearly-evaluation context) to test whether ev…
terms.full_def: Eval protocol that presents the model with a deployment-realistic context (or, conversely, a clearly-evaluation context) to test whether eval scores are stable across the eval-vs-deployment boundary. Motivated by the Apollo / scheming finding that models may behave differently when they suspect they are being evaluated
